news.Title: SpaceX Could Soon Be Worth $2 Trillion. This $66 Metric Is the One to Watch
news.Content:
Many investors are chomping at the bit to buy shares in a slew of upcoming IPOs, including OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX. The recent debut of the artificial intelligence semiconductor company Cerebras , which raised $5.5 billion from its IPO and saw its share price surge 68% on the first day of trading, shows just how eager some investors are for new tech stocks.

SpaceX -- which is run by Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) CEO Elon Musk -- is rapidly approaching its June 12 IPO and could raise up to $75 billion in the offering. That would give SpaceX a valuation of about $1.75 trillion to $2 trillion -- making it the biggest IPO ever .

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

And while those are unfathomable numbers, potential SpaceX investors may want to focus their attention instead on one declining number in the company's recent S-1 filing: The $66 in average revenue per user (ARPU) for the company's Starlink internet service.

Here's why.

Most companies try to expand ARPU, but SpaceX has other plans

While SpaceX is a rocket company with lofty ambitions to colonize Mars, it generates the vast majority of its revenue from its Starlink satellite internet business -- more than 60% in 2025.

Companies often work hard to improve their ARPU because higher revenue per user can mean more sales, potentially more profitability, and a higher return on their cost of acquisition. But in SpaceX's S-1 filing, the company showed that ARPU was declining and said it could fall further. Take a look:

2023

2024

2025

Q1 2026

Starlink average revenue per user (ARPU)

Source: SpaceX S-1 Filing.

From 2023 to the first quarter of this year, ARPU is down 33%. Usually, this would be a somewhat concerning trend. Explaining the decline, SpaceX's management said it expects ARPU to continue falling as it expands outside of the U.S., but that efficiencies will eventually balance out the decline:

"[W]e expect these dynamics to be offset by increased scale and technological advancement in our launch, satellite, and user terminal operations, ultimately supporting overall revenue growth and cost reduction."

In short, SpaceX is focusing on international customer expansion, which has lower ARPUs because the plans cost less. And the company is OK with that because it plans to increase efficiency to grow its margins.

Story Continues

I should mention that the company could stabilize, or even improve, its ARPU by expanding enterprise deals for its Starlink service. For example, the company has a growing list of airlines that pay for Starlink access to offer their passengers, and the U.S. government also has contracts for Starlink service.

But, at least for now, revenue per user is expected to fall.

Can SpaceX deliver on subscriber growth and efficiencies?

Investors only have a snapshot of SpaceX's finances, and while there are certainly some concerns about its spending, the company is making operational efficiency gains.

Consider that in 2025, SpaceX's Connectivity segment (which includes Starlink) saw its operating income spike 120% to $4.4 billion, and adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA) rose 86% to $7.1 billion. All of which occurred as Starlink ARPU was declining.

Additionally, the company's new Starship rocket can deploy 60 next-generation V3 satellites at once, a 20-fold increase over the capacity of its Falcon 9 rocket. What's more, SpaceX says its Falcon rockets have reduced launch costs by approximately 85% to 92% compared to historical averages, and the fully reusable Starship could push those reductions more.

Another metric where the company is clearly making progress that helps offset falling ARPU is through expanding Starlink customer numbers. Starlink customers surged 347% from just 2.3 million in 2023 to 10.3 million currently. And Starlink customers more than doubled in the past year alone.

That growth highlights just how good SpaceX is at attracting new customers, and the company believes its total addressable market (TAM) for connectivity is $1.6 trillion , including $870 billion from Starlink broadband and $740 billion from its Starlink mobile service.

Of course, having a large addressable market doesn't guarantee SpaceX will capture it .

Investors will have to keep a close watch

The SpaceX IPO will likely be massive. But if you buy shares, keep an eye on how quickly the company improves its efficiencies and watch Starlink's ARPUs.

If they keep falling and new operational efficiencies don't kick in, that could be a red flag.

So far, SpaceX has grown its customer base quickly, added new enterprise clients, and improved its operations to the point that declining ARPU may not be a big concern right now.

But SpaceX certainly has a lot of work to do to convince potential shareholders that it can eventually turn its growth into profitability.

We just issued 'double down' alerts on 3 stocks — find out if Tesla made our list

Ever feel like you missed the boat in buying the most successful stocks? Then you’ll want to hear this.

On rare occasions, our expert team of analysts issues a “Double Down” stock recommendation for companies that they think are about to pop. If you’re worried you’ve already missed your chance to invest, now is the best time to buy before it’s too late. And the numbers speak for themselves:

Nvidia: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2009, you’d have $557,318 !*

Apple: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2008, you’d have $58,766 !*

Netflix: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2004, you’d have $472,852 !*

Right now, we’re issuing “Double Down” alerts for three incredible companies , available when you join Stock Advisor , and there may not be another chance like this anytime soon.

See the 3 stocks »

*Stock Advisor returns as of May 26, 2026

Chris Neiger has no position in any of the stocks mentioned. The Motley Fool has positions in and recommends Tesla. The Motley Fool has a disclosure policy .

news.Title: The Stock Market Just Did Something for the 2nd Time in 100 Years, and History Says What Comes Next
news.Content:
The past two months have been nothing short of incredible for the stock market. At recent prices, the Dow Jones Industrial Average is up 12% since March 30, while the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) is up 18.5%, and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite is up a whopping 28% as artificial intelligence (AI) euphoria reaches a fever pitch.

But as exciting as the rally has been, there's a number that should give you pause: the Shiller CAPE ratio . The popular measure of how expensive the stock market is recently crossed 40 for just the second time in the last 100 years.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Here's what that means for investors.

What the Shiller CAPE ratio actually measures

The CAPE, or cyclically adjusted price-to-earnings ratio, is basically a smoothed-out price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio for the whole stock market. It takes the price of the S&P 500 and divides it by the average of its inflation-adjusted earnings over the past 10 years.

A standard P/E ratio can be distorted by a single great year or a single terrible one, but smoothing earnings over a decade strips out the noise and gives you a cleaner read on how much investors are actually paying.

When the ratio is high, it means the market is expensive relative to what companies have actually earned. When it's low, stocks are cheap in comparison. The historical average sits around 17, meaning the market is currently trading at more than double its long-run norm.

This is the highest the CAPE has been since the dot-com era

The only other time the CAPE ratio crossed 40 was in 1999, right at the peak of the dot-com market bubble . The S&P 500 had roughly tripled over the prior five years, driven by speculative enthusiasm for internet stocks that in many cases had no earnings at all.

Take a look at the CAPE over the last 100 years. Note: the data in the chart is slightly out of date, so the current reading above 40 isn't yet reflected here.

What history says about future returns

When the CAPE ratio is this elevated, returns have historically been poor. Research from Robert Shiller himself -- the Yale economist who created the metric -- shows that when the ratio is above 30, average annual returns over the following decade tend to land in the low single digits.

Above 40, there simply isn't enough data, but clearly, what exists isn't encouraging. The dot-com parallels are hard to ignore.

Story Continues

Just like during the dot-com era, today we have a market driven higher by extreme enthusiasm for a transformative technology and a belief that "it's different this time." We have stock valuations stretched well past what could reasonably be justified and a market that is essentially priced for perfection.

What should long-term investors do now?

Now, this by no means guarantees a crash is imminent or that the correction will be on the scale of 2000, but it is a cautionary tale.

Still, as much as history here seems to suggest a repeat of 2000 is in store, we can't know that with any certainty. And even if one is, you can be right about a crash and very wrong about when it comes, and the second question makes all the difference. Plenty of people predicted the dot-com bubble years before it actually burst, and they missed enormous gains in the meantime.

Timing the market is next to impossible, and trying to do so is risky. History's most important lesson here is that over the long haul, patient, steady investing has always been the winning formula.

news.Title: 川習會後 賴清德變了？  台股狂飆郭正亮曝有錢人開始做1件事
news.Content:
5月14日「川習會」後，美國總統川普直白說了「不希望看到有人走向獨立」，被解讀是向總統 賴清德 定調「不支持台獨」，但賴清德對兩岸交流依然維持冷處理，「隱性台獨」路線仍是台海和平一大隱憂。對此前立委 郭正亮 分析，賴清德現在已經不敢講台獨已經開始改說「拒統」；而台灣現在已經出現香港回歸前、1984年中英聯合聲明那種感覺，就是有錢人紛紛把資產外移、開始辦移民。

郭正亮今（28）日在YT節目「觀點 亮劍台灣」中指出，本來2027、2028年是非常危險的，因為你不知道賴清德會做什麼事，川習會後他現在已經不敢講台獨、不敢講統獨，怎麼辦？改說「拒統」，現在已經開始。「抗中保台」以後會改口叫「抗中拒統」。

郭正亮續指，台灣的有錢人不會跑掉嗎？當然會啊，跑得非常兇。郭分析，今年台灣開始出現1984年中英聯合聲明那種感覺。事實上香港在1984年前途就底定了，到1997年正式回歸有13年，可是資產開始外移1984年就開始了。台灣沒有「九七大限」，你如果好好做，兩岸不會發生戰爭的，結果賴清德搞到大家紛紛用腳投票。

郭正亮舉他住在大安區的朋友為例，觀察出一現象。他朋友在大安區也是民意代表，他說大安區現在身價一億的人上萬人，以前是把錢弄出去，今年是真的在辦移民。他們本來就是有錢人，這一波股市他們可能都賺到兩到三倍的錢，本來認為自己移民實力還不夠，今年因股市大漲，所以他們突然覺得自己賺夠了子彈，本來家底五千萬，現在變成一億五千萬，他就覺得他可以移民了。

接下來他講起台灣股市，「我說這個政府真的是非常可惡」，郭正亮說民進黨政府就跟大家講彭博的新聞報導，說台灣超過印度變成全世界第五大股市，很了不起，人口只有兩千將近四百萬但股市市值全球第五，美國當然第一大遙遙領先，第二大是中國大陸，可是我們這個政府從來不告訴你台灣股市有多畸形。

郭正亮分析，光是台積電就佔掉42%，「全世界有這種股市嗎？」沒有這種股市。英國現在已經跌到第九。美國第一大股叫NVIDIA（輝達），它佔市值只有6.7%，我們台灣是42%台積電。中國大陸排第二，總市值落後美國非常遠，美國78兆中國16.6兆，中國大陸第一大股值騰訊也只佔3.1%，佔比越低越好，表示什麼？各個行業利益均沾，產業多元化。跟台灣很像、排名第七的韓國，第一大企業三星佔總市值佔比是29%，還是低於台積電。

【看原文連結】

news.Title: 權值股翻黑壓盤，加權指數創高後急殺收低
news.Content:
【財訊快報／黃冠豪】台股今（28）日上演高檔劇烈震盪，早盤在美股創高、台積電ADR走強激勵下，加權指數一度再創盤中歷史新高。不過，隨著中東戰事傳來壞消息，美股期指盤中急挫，加上高檔獲利了結賣壓出籠，指數快速翻黑，終場大跌620.36點或1.4%，以43,636.44點作收，呈現明顯「創高後反轉」格局。亞洲股市今日同步陷入高檔整理。日經指數盤中震盪走弱，南韓KOSPI也在前一波AI與記憶體股大漲後轉為拉回。三星電子(005930.KS)股價同步回檔，雖然先前受到勞資協議進展與AI記憶體題材帶動，但短線漲幅過大後，資金明顯轉向獲利了結，也使韓股半導體族群氣氛降溫。

台股方面，早盤多頭氣勢一度旺盛，加權指數在台積電(2330)領軍下挑戰4萬5大關，盤中最高來到44,954.09點。不過，隨後賣壓急速升高，指數由大漲轉為急殺，盤中高低震盪近1,700點，終場未能守住紅盤。上櫃指數同樣由紅翻黑，顯示不僅大型權值股承壓，中小型題材股追價意願也明顯降溫。

權值股成為今日盤面轉弱關鍵。台積電早盤最高衝上2,360元，再創歷史新天價，原本扮演推升指數創高的核心角色，但午盤後買盤退潮，股價由高檔翻黑，成為拖累大盤的重要因素。聯電(2303)同樣開高走低，盤中雖一度受到晶圓代工題材支撐，但隨著大盤賣壓擴大，股價也由強轉弱，反映短線資金對前波急漲股開始採取落袋為安。

盤面熱門族群：矽晶圓族群早盤表現最為強勢，由環球晶(6488)領軍，股價一度衝上千元大關，帶動中美晶(5483)、合晶(6182)、嘉晶(3016)等同步表態。不過，隨著大盤急殺，族群漲幅明顯收斂，顯示資金仍偏短線操作。

被動元件族群方面，國巨*(2327)、華新科(2492)、凱美(2375)受惠漲價題材與AI伺服器需求想像，早盤仍有買盤點火，但盤中高檔震盪加劇，族群由強轉為分歧，國巨最終仍上漲5.85%。封測族群如日月光投控(3711)、京元電子(2449)、力成(6239)則受惠AI晶片測試與先進封裝需求支撐，盤中一度吸引資金關注，但在大盤轉弱後，漲勢同樣受到壓抑。

矽光子族群則全面走弱，包括聯亞(3081)、上詮(3363)、光聖(6442)、波若威(3163)等個股仍有買盤著墨。在整體盤勢轉空下，即使具備題材面的強勢股，也難以完全擺脫高檔震盪壓力。

整體來看，今日台股並非單純拉回，而是典型高檔爆量震盪後的反轉訊號。早盤創高代表多頭氣勢尚未完全退場，但午盤後急殺翻黑，顯示市場開始消化過熱籌碼。短線觀察重點，仍在台積電與聯電能否止穩，以及矽晶圓、被動元件、封測、矽光子等熱門族群是否能維持輪動。若權值股無法快速回穩，指數恐進一步測試短期均線支撐，操作上宜由追價轉為觀察量縮止跌訊號。

news.Title: 輝達隔壁開大會、富豪在天母忙傳承！台股創1.55兆天量，高資產客卻不為所動？渣打游天立親揭他們怎麼想
news.Content:
週三(5/27)在台北市的士林地區，一邊是輝達（NVIDIA）於北士科新總部預定地舉行台灣員工大會，另一邊則有渣打銀行於中山北路七段上宣布「天母國際私人理財中心」開幕。

渣打銀行積極布局台灣財富管理市場，渣打集團財富管理暨消費金融業務兼東協、南亞、大中華及北亞區行政總裁徐仲薇，在週三親自出席剪彩活動，並於致詞時感性表示：「台灣是我出生的地方，所以對我來說永遠都非常溫馨，我們在這裡的業務也越來越好。」

看好台灣潛力 18個月連開3國際私人理財中心

看好台灣財富管理市場的龐大需求，渣打銀行在通路布局上展現強大雄心，在18個月之內開出3家國際私人理財中心。繼去年2月在台中七期開設首站、7月插旗台北信義區松高後，第三家中心則選在國際化的天母地區落腳。

渣打集團財富管理暨消費金融業務兼東協、南亞、大中華及北亞區行政總裁徐仲薇。（攝影：林韋伶）

徐仲薇表示，亞洲是全球財富管理市場的重要成長動能，而台灣客戶的財富管理需求也正快速與國際接軌，「客戶對財富管理的期待正在改變。從資產配置、跨境金融服務到數位化體驗，缺一不可；高資產客戶也更加重視值得信賴的專業建議，以及能貼近生活方式的財富管理方案。渣打全球網絡與國際資源不僅切合客戶的需求與市場發展趨勢，我們更高興在台灣同步推出完整的財富管理方案，凸顯台灣位居集團前三大財富管理市場的戰略佈局。」

渣打銀行總經理游天立則表示，總部一直視台灣為最重要的市場之一。渣打在外商銀行中率先推出亞資中心所開放的產品，未來將持續引進最專業、國際化的商品與訓練有素的團隊，提供頂級客群最好的服務。

搶攻頂級客 第三季將推出頂級金屬卡

為了全方位滿足高資產客戶，游天立也在現場首度曝光專屬的頂級卡片「世界之極Beyond Card」，他表示，為了進一步打造高資產客群的不凡體驗，渣打將於第三季推出邀請制的「渣打Beyond世界之極卡」，讓優先私人理財貴賓的尊榮延伸至國際化的生活場景。

圖為渣打銀行「世界之極Beyond Card」。（照片來源：渣打銀行提供）

該信用卡著重在環球旅行與頂級餐飲禮遇、採金屬卡面設計，不僅呼應高資產客群對品味與細節的高度要求，優先禮遇更涵蓋日常消費，搭配專屬的專業諮詢與全球金融支援，從國內外行程規劃、餐飲與消費、全球機場接送、貴賓室攜伴與快速通關服務，到國際私人理財中心的專屬禮賓服務，無縫銜接。

面對台股熱潮 富豪客戶仍重視配置

談到近期全球股市持續創高

news.Title: 台股盤中創高後跳水 終場大跌620點失守44K 單日震盪逾1700點創紀錄
news.Content:
台股今（28）日開高走低，加權指數盤中最高一度衝上44954.09點，刷新歷史新高，不過隨後指數由紅翻黑，盤中最低重挫逾千點，下探43236.36點，單日高低震盪超過1700點，終場大跌620.36點，收在43636.44點，44K關卡失守，成交量達1兆5904.76億。

護國神山台積電（2330）盤中一度飆上2360元歷史天價，不過尾盤漲勢收斂，終場下跌5元，收在2295元，跌幅0.22%。

其他權值股表現方面，鴻海（2317）下跌1元，收在263元，跌幅0.38%；聯發科（2454）重挫230元，收在4410元，跌幅4.96%；廣達（2382）下跌3.5元，收在308元，跌幅1.12%；台達電（2308）大跌130元，收在2390元，跌幅5.16%；台光電（2383）下跌250元，收在5055元，跌幅4.71%。

市場分析指出，台股近期漲幅已大，加上高檔獲利了結賣壓出籠，又因國際戰事傳出再度升溫，導致盤中震盪加劇，後續仍需觀察國際股市走勢及資金動向。

責任編輯／蔡尚晉

news.Title: 6月台股期指跌952點
news.Content:
（中央社台北28日電）台北股市今天終場跌620.36點，收43636.44點。6月台指期以43842點作收，下跌952點，與現貨相較，正價差205.56點。

台灣期貨交易所為近2個月指數期貨契約訂定開盤參考價為，6月期貨44794點、7月期貨44800點。

台股今天終場跌620.36點，收43636.44點，成交金額新台幣1兆5904.76億元。

6月台指期貨以43842點作收，跌952點，成交9萬7890口；7月期貨以43865點作收，跌935點，成交841口。實際收盤價格以期交所公告為準。1150528

news.Title: 早盤創高後翻黑「狂震逾1700點」！台股終場下跌620點
news.Content:
即時中心／綜合報導

美股三大指數週三（27日）同創歷史新高，台股今（28）日早盤來歷史高點44,954.09點，有望一舉站上4萬5大關；然而賣壓大舉出籠，稍早更傳出伊朗突襲美軍基地，盤中翻黑向下跌破4萬4，一路重挫至43,236.36點，高低震盪1,717.73點。尾盤逐步拉升，終場指數就以就以43,636.44點作收、下跌620.36點，跌幅1.40％，成交量1兆5,904.76億元。台積電（2330）盤中創2,360元新天價後也翻黑，股價終場下跌5元，以2,295元作收。

電子權值股方面，鴻海（2317）收在263元，跌1元；聯發科（2454）收4,410元，下跌230元；廣達（2382）收308.5元，跌3.5元；台達電（2308）以2,390元作收，下滑130元；日月光投控（3711）跌15元，收627元；聯電（2303）下跌1.5元，收142元；大立光（3008）收在3,425元，下滑80元。

金融方面，富邦金（2881）收在110元平盤價；國泰金（2882）收在84.8元，跌0.6元；中信金（2891）以59.3元作收，漲0.1元；兆豐金（2886）收在39.8元，漲0.35元；華南金（2880）收29.6元，下滑0.35元；第一金（2892）收27.25元，跌0.35元；合庫金（5880）以22.7元作收，小跌0.05元。

今日成交量前10名個股依序為群創、力積電、友達、聯電、華邦電、彩晶、泰金寶-DR、南亞科、旺宏、台新新光金。此外，漲幅前五名為大億、耿鼎、倉佑、虹揚-KY、時碩工業；至於跌幅前五名則為創意、睿生光電、麗嬰房、森崴能源、高力。

news.Title: Exclusive-ByteDance developing custom CPU chips to support AI rollout, sources say
news.Content:
By Liam Mo and Fanny Potkin

BEIJING/PARIS, May 28 (Reuters) - Chinese technology giant ByteDance is developing its own central processing units (CPUs) to support its growing AI infrastructure needs, three people familiar with the matter said, as surging chip prices and prolonged supply ‌shortages constrain its expansion plans.

More from Yahoo Scout

How are CPU prices impacting ByteDance's costs?

What chip architectures is ByteDance considering?

How are CPU shortages affecting the industry?

Why is ByteDance developing its own CPUs?

The move underscores the industry's rapid shift toward "inference," where AI models are deployed to perform agentic tasks ‌that demand more from CPUs, working in tandem with the graphics chips made by Nvidia that have dominated the AI boom.

The shift has created a shortage of CPUs in recent ​months, and global hyperscalers including Alphabet's Google, Amazon and Microsoft are also developing their own custom CPUs to reduce costs and tailor performance to their specific workloads. It has also helped major CPU makers Intel and AMD emerge as leading challengers to Nvidia's AI dominance.

ByteDance, the parent of short video platform TikTok, is targeting deployment of its proprietary CPU in its own servers and data centres to support internal operations, as it prepares a massive rollout of ‌agent-based products including its Coze platform, the first source ⁠said.

The Beijing-based company has approached several external partners to assist with the effort, and those partners are expected to contribute not only to the chip's design work but also to help secure manufacturing capacity at foundries, the sources ⁠added. The project remains at an early stage, the first source said.

They declined to be named, as the plan is not public.

ByteDance did not respond to Reuters' request for comment.

CPU SHORTAGE

ByteDance's move places it alongside a growing cohort of tech companies that have concluded the economics of custom chips outweigh the complexity of ​designing them.

It ​is pursuing two chip architecture tracks for its CPU development — one based on ​SoftBank-owned Arm and another on the open-source RISC-V instruction set ‌architecture, as it weighs which design best suits its long-term data centre requirements, the sources said.

Developing two designs simultaneously is a common hedge for technology giants, as it allows them to test their options before committing to a costly, large-scale manufacturing run.

Arm did not respond immediately to a request for comment.

The push to develop proprietary silicon comes as Intel has warned Chinese customers of server CPU delivery lead times of up to six months, Reuters reported in February.

Intel said last month that demand for its CPU from AI firms was so strong in the first quarter that it ‌sold even chips it had originally written off.

Story Continues

AMD CEO Lisa Su warned last week ​that the global CPU market is "tight," with demand outpacing forecasts and supply constraints expected to ​persist.

ByteDance currently sources CPUs from Intel and AMD, and they ​have raised prices significantly, with quarter-over-quarter increases ranging from 10% to as much as 35% in recent months, two ‌of the sources said, prompting ByteDance to accelerate its push ​for in-house alternatives.

Intel said it had updated ​prices on some of its products to reflect sustained demand, increased component and material costs and evolving market dynamics. AMD did not respond immediately to a request for comment.

Nvidia is expanding beyond GPUs into the CPU market, and its CEO Jensen Huang hopes its ​new "Vera" central processors will give the firm access to ‌a new $200 billion market.

It unveiled a new central processor and AI system built on technology from Groq - a chip startup ​specialising in inference - in March, making moves to defend its position in the AI chip market.

(Reporting by Liam Mo in Beijing ​and Fanny Potkin in Paris; Editing by Miyoung Kim and Jamie Freed)

news.Title: 台股創高後崩殺1700點！收盤下跌620點 2大原因釀暴跌
news.Content:
台股今（27）日劇烈波動，最高一度衝上44954.09點，再創歷史新高。然而，由於短線漲幅過大，加上市場傳出美伊戰事再度升溫，2大原因湧現市場賣壓，台股隨後由紅翻黑，盤中最低崩千點，下探43236.36點，單日高低震盪逾1700點。台股終場大跌620.36點收43636.44點，44K關卡失守。

台積電一度衝上2360元天價，但隨後翻黑，終場跌5元收2295元。被列注意股的台達電及聯發科，分別下殺5.16%及4.96%，聯電今早衝上151元，但也翻黑，收142元。國巨不畏盤勢動盪續漲逾5%，帶動被動元件股強漲。記憶體股漲跌互見，連2根漲停的華邦電開殺，下跌7.1%，南亞科、旺宏逆勢漲逾2.5%。

市場傳出美國與伊朗之間戰事再起，《路透社》引述美軍官員消息最新報導指出，美軍對一處伊朗軍事設施實施新的打擊，攔截擊落了多架無人機，中東局勢突然升溫拖累亞洲股市急挫。

美股道瓊上漲182.60點或0.36%，收50,644.28點。S&P500上漲1.24點或0.02%，收7,520.36點。NASDAQ上漲18.55點或0.07%，收26,674.74點。費城半導體下跌174.72點或1.36%，收12,702.19點。

news.Title: 台股再創高 ETF也瘋了！「不配息ETF」009816站穩15元 上市三個半月累積漲幅狂飆51%
news.Content:
商傳媒 ｜記者陳宜靖／台北報導

台股加權指數在 AI 供應鏈與半導體權值股領軍下，持續刷新歷史紀錄，資金也加速流向追蹤大盤特性的市值型ETF。全台首檔主打不配息再投入設計的「凱基台灣 TOP 50（009816）」，近期隨大盤走勢攻勢凌厲，股價已正式站穩 15 元整數關卡，不僅成為市場焦點，更在短短上市3個半月內，交出累積漲幅超過51%的驚人表現，吸引大量投資人搶進。

根據臺灣證券交易所最新統計，009816 的資產規模已快速擴張至 1,336.72 億元，總受益人數同步來到 78.26 萬人，成為今年ETF市場人氣竄升速度最快的產品之一。

回顧 009816 的績效表現，自2月初以 10 元發行價掛牌上市以來，歷經約三個半月的市場實測，截至最新數據的累計漲幅已高達 51% 以上。市場分析指出，此波強勁的漲幅除了受惠於台股突破 44,000 點的多頭行情外，也與該基金選股邏輯有關，不僅聚焦台股50強，選股條件更加入企業近四季稅後純益總和大於0的機制，不納入市值大但不賺錢的「虛胖企業」進入投資組合。

另外，009816採取「不配息」設計，與傳統高股息ETF不同，不將股息配發給投資人，而是直接留在基金內再投入，希望透過時間與複利效果，持續放大淨值成長，「讓收益繼續留在市場滾大」，也成為近年ETF市場的重要趨勢。

隨著台股進入高波動、高成長的新時代，「不配息複利型ETF」正逐漸崛起。尤其在AI產業長線趨勢持續發酵下，009816兼具市值型ETF穩定性與長期資本增值概念，也讓市場開始重新定義ETF投資邏輯。

news.Title: 台股過熱了嗎？童子賢罕見成「股市童老師」　給出「4字」定心丸
news.Content:
從來不評論股市與股價的和碩董事長 童子賢 ，今日罕見化身為「童老師」為擔心台股過熱的股民開示，他認為，從總經的角度來看，台股全年本益比只要控制在25～30倍之間，就沒有過熱的疑慮，他推算今年全年台股本益比大概25倍左右，相比於全球很熱的股市來說，台灣不算過熱、「不用擔心」。

近來美股與台股頻創新高，台股市值已經超越印度、躍居全球第五大市場，許多股民擔心股市會不會過熱。童子賢在股東會後主動提出他對台股的觀察，他認為，2000年時網際網路曾經因為過熱而崩盤，不過時至今日，沒有網際網路日子都不用過了，台灣瞄準的產業可以增值所有的數位裝置，對全球經濟有幫助，台灣更是處於AI潮流的中心，AI浪潮不會一、兩年就結束，現在的台股有它的價值。

他評估，就總體經濟與歷史紀錄來說，台股本益比若在25～30倍之間就沒有過熱的疑慮，以第一季台灣整體上市櫃公司獲利為1.6兆元來推算，今年全年台灣整體上市櫃公司獲利將超過6兆元，換算下來，今年台股的本益比大約是25倍左右，如果股市繼續大漲可能也才只有28倍，都不算過熱，而且在台灣股市交易的外資，也因為在台灣有辦理公司登記而在台灣繳稅、聘請台灣員工，都有帶動台灣的經濟跟就業。

至於台股不少個股本益比遠超過25倍，他認為這代表個別企業的前景被高度看好的結果，跟台股整體有沒有過熱無關。

面對台灣有些批評聲浪認為，股市大好卻只嘉惠到電子股，童子賢則認為，現在小小一個手提箱裡面裝的晶片，產值就遠超過一個大卡車裝的成衣，「難道我們要回到40年前出口成衣的日子嗎？」台灣面積不大，生產附加價值高的產品比較合適。

回到原文

news.Title: 台股盤中屠殺1020點！電零股兩樣情「臻鼎-KY亮綠燈、鴻名排隊搶買」　股民哀：我歐印在山頂
news.Content:
[ FTNN新聞網 ]記者薛明峻／台北報導

台股今（28）日早盤在台積電帶動下，加權指數大漲697.29點，衝上44954.09點再創新高，不過融資餘額過高，加上美伊突然傳出發動新一輪攻擊，脆弱的停火協議恐生變，指數盤中跳水下殺至43236.36點，大跌1020.44點，股民們也哀鴻遍野，「最近天氣熱，趟在地上涼涼」、「漲一千擔心漲多了，跌一千世界末日」、「我今天歐印在山頂」、「空頭大屠殺，這只是剛開始！」

五大電子權值股中，台積電（2330）早盤一度攻上2360元，大漲60元，漲幅2.61%，但盤中下挫30元至2270元，跌幅1.30%；台達電（2308）開低走低，盤中下探2370元，大跌5.95%；聯發科（2454）盤中殺至4270元，崩跌7.97%；鴻海（2317）開高走低，盤中最低來到260元，下跌1.52%；日月光投控（3711）盤中也下探617元，崩跌3.89%。

電子零組件股盤中帶頭下殺，臻鼎-KY（4958）一度來到479元亮燈跌停、日電貿（3090）也逼近跌停，嘉基（6715）大跌8%；華通（2313）、南電（8046）、鉅祥（2476）、聯德控股-KY（4912）跌7%；欣興（3037）、聯茂（6213）、嘉澤（3533）、志聖（2467）、健鼎（3044）、天二科技（6834）跌6%；台光電（2383）、達邁（3645）、金像電（2368）、建準（2421）、榮科（4989）等跌幅也有半根。

不過仍有多檔個股強勢漲停，包括凱美（2375）、興勤（2428）、立隆電（2472）、鴻名（3021）、晶技（3042）、柏承（6141）、鈞寶（6155）、聚鼎（6224）共8檔盤中仍強鎖漲停，其中鴻名高掛1.6萬張買單排隊搶進中，今日拉出第5根漲停，表現強勢。

news.Title: 人民幣走強+台股翻黑，台幣早盤小升1.5分
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-05-28 13:06:24 陳怡潔 發佈

今(28)日人民幣中間價衝上逾三年新高，激勵亞洲貨幣走勢，台幣兌美元早盤則連續第七日走升，不過台股盤中翻黑下殺，台幣升幅亦受限，中午則小幅升值1.5分，暫收31.405元。

美軍27日對伊朗發動新一輪攻勢後，伊朗也鎖定美軍基地攻擊報復；美伊局勢反覆，今日在東京匯市早盤，美元指數持穩、守在99.288附近，不過，人民幣再度衝高，今日人民幣兌美元中間價升值51基點至6.8240元，創下逾三年新高，帶動離岸、在岸人民幣同步走升。

人民幣中間價衝上逾三年新高，激勵亞洲貨幣走勢，台幣兌美元早盤震盪走升、為連續第七日上揚，不過，台股今日開高、一度創下歷史新高44954.09點後，盤中翻黑下殺，台幣升幅亦受到壓抑，中午則小幅升值1.5分，暫收31.405元。

根據台北外匯經紀公司報價，28日早盤台幣兌美元升值1.5分，暫收31.405元，區間為31.379-31.428元，成交8.58億美元。

台股再衝新高 台幣早盤爆量強升1.52角

股匯續齊揚 台幣早盤放量升值6.6分

news.Title: Marvell Technology Inc (MRVL) Q1 2027 Earnings Call Highlights: Record Revenue and Strategic ...
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Revenue: $2.418 billion, 28% year-over-year growth, 9% sequential growth.

Data Center Revenue: $1.83 billion, 11% sequential growth, 27% year-over-year growth.

Non-GAAP Earnings Per Share: $0.80, exceeded guidance midpoint by $0.01.

GAAP Gross Margin: 52.1%.

Non-GAAP Gross Margin: 58.9%.

GAAP Operating Expenses: $921 million.

Non-GAAP Operating Expenses: $577 million.

GAAP Operating Margin: 14%.

Non-GAAP Operating Margin: 35%.

Cash Flow from Operations: $639 million.

Total Debt: $4.96 billion.

Guidance for Q2 Fiscal 2027 Revenue: $2.7 billion, 12% sequential growth, 35% year-over-year growth at midpoint.

Guidance for Q2 Fiscal 2027 Non-GAAP Earnings Per Share: $0.88 to $0.98.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 9 Warning Signs with MRVL.

Is MRVL fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Release Date: May 27, 2026

For the complete transcript of the earnings call, please refer to the full earnings call transcript .

Positive Points

Marvell Technology Inc ( NASDAQ:MRVL ) delivered record revenue of $2.418 billion for the first quarter of fiscal 2027, reflecting 9% sequential and 28% year-over-year growth.

The company expects total revenue to grow 12% sequentially and 35% year over year in the second quarter, with a projection of reaching $3 billion in quarterly revenue by Q3, one quarter ahead of prior expectations.

Marvell's data center business is a significant growth driver, expected to grow approximately 50% in fiscal 2027, with the interconnect business projected to grow more than 70% year over year.

The expanded partnership with NVIDIA is expected to create new market opportunities, leveraging Marvell's custom silicon and optical networking capabilities.

Marvell's acquisition of Polariton and Celestial AI enhances its technology platform, positioning the company to lead in high-speed, low-power silicon photonics and scale-up optics markets.

Negative Points

GAAP earnings per diluted share were $0.04 lower than guidance due to purchase accounting impacts from recent acquisitions.

The communications and other end markets are expected to decline in the mid-single-digit range sequentially in the second quarter.

Marvell's GAAP operating expenses are projected to increase to approximately $960 million in the second quarter.

The company is facing capacity constraints in AI-related products, necessitating $1 billion in prepayments to secure future supply.

Despite strong growth projections, the company acknowledges potential risks and uncertainties that could impact future performance.

Story Continues

Q & A Highlights

Q : Matt, you mentioned your custom XPU is on target to hit $10 billion in fiscal '29. Can you confirm this, and when will you feel more comfortable discussing the large customer and the progress in this new program? A : Yes, you heard it right. We are tracking to achieve over $10 billion in fiscal '29, driven by our data center custom silicon market. The program remains on track, and we expect to see significant growth in our custom business next year. We will provide more updates as we progress through the year.

Q : With the evolution of inference and compute workloads, is Marvell leveraging its SRAM-based IP for XPU offload ASICs, and do you have design wins for SRAM-based offload architectures? A : Yes, our SRAM design capability is a key part of our IP portfolio and is crucial for winning designs in XPU attach. This technology has evolved from networking products into AI products, and we continue to see it as a trend in the market.

Q : Can you discuss the breadth of your customer base and whether you are moving into the compute TAM? A : We have custom engagements across all U.S. hyperscalers, including XPU and XPU attach. Our growth projections are based on designs we've already won, and we are competing for the most important sockets in the world. Our technology platform is very competitive, and we are well-positioned for future opportunities.

Q : How are you addressing capacity constraints, and is the increase in guidance due to more capacity or better customer forecasts? A : We have built tight relationships with key suppliers, providing them with a 5-year forecast and making strategic prepayments to ensure capacity. The increase in guidance reflects both securing more capacity and becoming more comfortable with customer forecasts.

Q : Can you provide more color on the interconnect business, specifically scale-up optics, and what has been surprising in terms of product demand? A : We have the broadest range of connectivity solutions in the industry. The most intense activity has been in scale-up optics, where we offer end-to-end solutions from XPU to switch. Our acquisition of Celestial AI has strengthened our capabilities, and we are seeing significant interest in our comprehensive system solutions.

news.Title: 童子賢：台股本益比全年控制在20至30倍不算過熱 現階段AI發展不到30%
news.Content:
台股屢創新高，市場擔心出現過熱跡象。和碩董事長 童子賢 今天（28日）罕見對股市發表看法，他認為關鍵指標是本益比，台股目前本益比約22到28倍，若全年控制在20到30倍，不算過熱；他表示，台灣處在AI潮流的中心，現階段AI發展連30%都不到，還在持續發展中。

童子賢上午在和碩股東會後接受媒體採訪指出，目前人工智慧（AI）產業趨勢對未來全球經濟有幫助，類似當初網際網路的出現，在未來幾年會逐漸發酵，只要有數位裝置的地方，都可以增進效能和更智慧化，希望是一個良性循環。

他強調，台灣處在這波潮流的中心，不是一、兩年就會結束，現階段AI的發展，可能連30%都不到。

童子賢說，台灣有努力也有成果，這是幸運的。有人問他台股是否過熱？擔心上漲都集中在科技股？他反問，「難道我們要回到40年前出口成衣的日子？」能做高附加價值的產品，其實還是挺不錯。

市場傳出和碩子公司景碩獲AI晶片大廠輝達（NVIDIA）入股？童子賢澄清並無此事。入股方式之一，是在現貨市場買股票，但對公司沒有營運上的幫助，因為資金轉移到另一個股東；另一種方式是公司發行新股認購，目前為止沒有這件事。

童子賢分析，從產業現況來看，比較常見方式，是預付款或設備資助，由客戶先付訂金，確保產能，或直接購買專用設備，放到工廠，目的在於鼓勵供應鏈擴產、降低長期風險。

談到台美關稅議題，童子賢表示，從產業角度當然是希望安定，希望台灣處於有利的競爭位置，今天公布的內容是排除半導體的關稅，對台灣有明確規範，其實是不錯的。台灣的出口為需求驅動，並非低價傾銷或不當擴產，也未涉及不公平競爭或國安疑慮，應該不會觸犯相關條款。

至於6月1日將參加輝達GTC Taipei主題演講前的暖場活動，童子賢說，這是年度盛會，過去幾年都是潮流和目光焦點所在，他會接受訪問，向大家報告參與這個潮流的心情、與和碩努力的方向，自己只是扮演參與者之一。

（責任主編：莊儱宇）

news.Title: 台股狂飆卻先獲利了結？她認「將資金慢慢放口袋」 內行人曝：不要預測漲跌
news.Content:
[ FTNN新聞網 ]實習記者鄭巧薰／綜合報導

台股近期氣勢如虹，不少個股接連亮燈漲停。不過，卻有一名網友坦言，自己近期已開始陸續將資金「放口袋」，雖然成功獲利了結，但每天看到一堆飆股狂漲，內心仍忍不住感到煎熬，貼文曝光後引發不少投資人共鳴。

一名網友在Dcard以「有人也慢慢把資金放口袋嗎？」為題發文表示，雖然目前市場趨勢看起來仍處於大多頭階段，但漲勢強勁到「完全沒有要休息的感覺」，也讓他開始好奇，是否真的有人已經離職專職炒股。

不過原PO坦言，自己其實已經提前將大部分資金獲利了結，「我先承認自己很笨，已經把大部分資金放口袋了」，如今天天看著市場上一檔又一檔亮燈漲停的股票，讓他忍不住感嘆「心有點疼」。

貼文曝光後，不少網友也分享自身看法，有人認為「調節 寧願少賺 也不要一根下殺時跑不掉」、「我也是上個月開始慢慢放口袋了，現在剩兩成左右」、「我覺得寧願少賺也要活得久」、「至少一半資金進 不要預測漲跌 現在股市很瘋不知道能多瘋」。

另有部分網友則認為，目前市場過熱，「沒有遠見，那就先專心吃眼前的飯，投資需要眼光，眼光需要時間與經驗去培養，入袋為安也是種策略，盲目追高被套牢那就得不償失」、「入袋才算數，帳面上看到股票都是估值，賣出才是自己的，沒賣都是虛」、「不要預設高點，但要自己有交易策略」，提醒原PO保留現金未必是壞事。

news.Title: 早盤暴漲700、午盤狂瀉900！主力狂拉台積電背後藏殺機「專家點名3類人最慘」短線最強避風港看低基期股
news.Content:
受到美股與台積電ADR創新高的激勵，台積電（2330）飆上2,360元的歷史新天價，台股今（28）日早盤暴漲逾700點，然午盤後利調節賣壓傾巢而出，驚見大跳水急殺，一度急殺超過900點，盤中上下劇烈震盪高達1,600點，爆出天量換手；資深證券分析師認為，早盤大漲完全是「主力拉高台積電、聯發科」的假象，中小型股當時早已大面積失血。

摩爾投顧分析師林漢偉指出，台積電強勢飆上2,360元新天價、加權指數逼近45,000點大關，在技術面與心理面都屬於極端過熱區，由於消費者對股票投資的信心已降至低點，市場普遍認為當前估值過高，導致早盤追高資金一見苗頭不對，便轉為瘋狂反手拋售。

早盤的暴漲幾乎全靠少數半導體鏈或權值股在撐盤，盤面結構其實已經極端分歧，午盤當資金無法持續外溢到其他族群、且主要指標性權值股轉弱時，市場缺乏實質承接買盤，導致指數如同失去支撐般迅速崩跌，更進一步引發程式交易停損單與當沖客退場的連鎖踩踏效應。

前波漲勢凌厲的指標股在盤中遭遇瘋狂拋售，其中聯發科（2454）早盤衝高後遭到爆砍，午盤重挫逾7％；台達電（2308）下跌超過4％；高價股如智邦（2345）也大跌逾7％，原本早盤短暫出現的「53千金」奇景淪為曇花一現，高檔換手演變成結構性多殺多。

這種資金極度集中的「不健康拉積盤」，導致午盤權值股一旦鬆動，中小型股便率先崩盤，進而引發全面性的多頭踩踏。在指數逼近歷史天價且成交量暴增至歷史天量的敏感時刻，「不會在市場最熱的時候把自己押滿」，短期內大盤需要時間沉澱籌碼，投資人此時應嚴格執行移動停損點，提高現金部位以靜待盤勢止穩。

投資專家指出，早盤暴漲700點、午盤倒跌800點，此種極端的上沖下洗，會在短時間內擊潰不堅定的散戶、當沖客與融資高槓桿資金，然在台積電與AI鏈大跌時，力積電（6770）、南亞科（2408）等記憶體族群反而逆勢大漲，資金並未完全逃離股市，而是在內部進行「高檔轉低檔」的良性大輪動。

早盤追高的強勢股如AI鏈、千金股等，若午盤後帶量跌破關鍵技術指標，例如5日線、3日低點，必須果斷停損，防止資金被套牢在歷史天價區，若想承接優質基本面個股，應放棄一次性梭哈，改用分批逢低承接或定期定額，攤平時間成本，短線可聚焦低基期記憶體，以及具備護盤色彩的金控、傳產雙低股，其表現相對抗跌。

news.Title: 聯發科前進COMPUTEX　展示邊緣AI和雲端方案
news.Content:
（中央社記者張建中新竹2026年5月28日電）IC設計廠聯發科 (2454) 今天宣布，將在COMPUTEX 2026展出邊緣人工智慧（AI）到雲端的次世代技術與解決方案，包括WiFi 8系列產品、6G、衛星通訊以及先進資料中心等前瞻技術。

聯發科發布新聞稿，總經理陳冠州表示，聯發科在代理式AI趨勢下，從邊緣端至雲端都擁有絕佳優勢，不僅為各類邊緣裝置提供算力，並布局雲端資料中心技術，同時持續推進無縫串聯邊緣與雲端的通訊技術。

陳冠州說，聯發科將持續攜手全球夥伴、客戶、AI生態系及半導體供應鏈，加速實現無所不在的AI，並進一步擴大AI基礎設施投資。

聯發科表示，將與輝達（NVIDIA）在COMPUTEX中，共同展出搭載NVIDIA GB10 Grace Blackwell超級晶片的代理式AI（Agentic AI）超級電腦NVIDIA DGXSpark，能在裝置上直接運作大型AI模型，以自動且智慧協調執行任務，並重新定義桌上型裝置的智慧生產力。

此外，聯發科也會展出搭載NVIDIA G-SYNC Pulsar技術顯示控制晶片的電競螢幕。以及天璣座艙旗艦平台C-X1，其整合NVIDIA AI與遊戲技術，支援AgenticAI與感知運算整合、邊緣與雲端混合運算需求，將其打造為主動式AI智慧座艙，讓座艙系統從被動的工具升格為懂車、懂乘客的得力智慧助手。

聯發科指出，現場還將展出天璣汽車連結旗艦平台MT2739，支援3GPP R18 5G NR-NTN衛星通話，可以實現從世界任一角落皆能用視訊通話無縫溝通的體驗，並降低訊號切換卡頓30%，讓視訊會議、地圖軟體等應用體驗在駛入地下室或隧道瞬間依舊不受影響。

聯發科也將展出多款支援高階運算的應用，如支援離線AI生成的手機與平板平台、支援AI生產力應用的Chromebook平台、支援5K AI畫質提升的寬螢幕電競顯示控制晶片平台、支援商用無人機、自主移動機器人、可運作OpenClaw模型的物聯網平台等。

聯發科資料中心解決方案不僅提供單一零組件，更涵蓋端到端的AI資料中心平台。從客製化特殊應用晶片（ASIC）與XPU設計、2.5D和3.5D先進封裝技術、高速互連技術，到機櫃層級整合，將AI擴展的底層架構創新整合至單一系統，協助客戶大規模部署AI資料中心。

至於矽光子方面，聯發科將展出400Gbps頻寬速率的共同封裝光學（CPO）技術，以及可應用於資料中心互連主動式光纜（AOC）的MicroLED光學技術應用。

聯發科將展示能夠跨世代互通的WiFi 8晶片組，同時擁有3GPP R18與WiFi 8功能的T930 5G固定無線接取（FWA）平台，以及6G裝置協作多天線（Co-MIMO）技術。

news.Title: 聯發科助攻馬斯克？有望成特斯拉AI超級晶圓廠夥伴  郭明錤揭「隱形實力」狂加分
news.Content:
聯發科獲得Google大單，引爆新一波外資調升估值行情，摩根士丹利（大摩）日前率先將聯發科目標價調高至5088元新天價。與此同時，天風國際證券分析師郭明錤釋出最新產業調查指出，聯發科可能成為特斯拉執行長馬斯克（Elon Musk）打造「Terafab（超級晶圓廠）」的關鍵客製化晶片（ASIC）策略合作夥伴。

聯發科可能成馬斯克最佳戰友三關鍵

郭明錤在社群平台 X 以「聯發科可能是Elon Musk打造Terafab的關鍵拼圖」為題發文指出，在數家客製化ASIC廠商的激烈競爭中，聯發科因具備獨特優勢，目前看來較有可能成為Terafab的策略合作對象。這項合作將由聯發科出面，全面支援英特爾14A先進製程與先進封裝的導入與生產，預計自2028年開始小量生產馬斯克的IC設計團隊所需晶片。

此策略合作可望強化Terafab的執行效率，並提升聯發科AI ASIC的產業定位與附加價值。

郭明錤表示，馬斯克旗下的IC設計團隊目前正同時面臨產品線、時間與人力等多重執行面壓力。由於該團隊是首次與英特爾合作，並採用其14A先進製程與先進封裝技術，因此急需適當的ASIC廠商協助加速技術導入。在數家競爭者中，聯發科因具備三大核心優勢，被評為Terafab的最優選擇。

第一，聯發科同時具有投片英特爾前段製程（Intel 16）與參與先進封裝（EMIB-T）的實戰經驗，因更熟悉與英特爾的合作模式，能協助Terafab精準掌握14A PDK 0.9釋出後的數月黃金窗口與後續製造。

第二，聯發科與Google首度合作的TPU晶片開發進度超乎預期，其中「8t」將於2026年第四季量產、「Humufish」則於2027年下半年量產，這證明聯發科具備Semi-COT模式、EMIB-T生產協同能力與Tier-1（一線大廠）量級的量產經驗。

第三，聯發科旗下的MT7629與MT7762/61系列，原本就是低軌衛星星鏈（Starlink）用戶端的Wi-Fi與路由器晶片供應商；在高時程壓力下，馬斯克團隊選擇已通過驗證的既有夥伴，將能大幅加速研發進展。

美台跨時區換手研發 在蘋果供應鏈中獲得驗證

郭明錤提到，聯發科背後另一個加分項，即台灣半導體生態系的加速研發模式。

他分析，台積電之所以成功，24/7輪班研發模式的夜鷹計畫是該公司提升執行效率的關鍵之一，連韓國政界與半導體業界也曾呼籲政府應修法並仿效台灣。這也意味著聯發科的價值，不僅在於研發能量，更在於能將一整套「台灣特有的加速研發生態系」導入Terafab。

蘋果（Apple）供應鏈已經在善用此加速優勢。某美國領導上游材料商為滿足Apple新產品開發節奏，在台灣設立實驗室並仿效夜鷹模式，至今成效讓Apple感到滿意。若Terafab與聯發科合作並善用台灣的加速研發優勢，在美台兩地搭配跨時區換手研發，當可強化其執行效率。

news.Title: 大怒神！台股創高後狂震1500點 台積電下殺翻黑
news.Content:
台股今（27）日上演大幅震盪行情，最高一度衝上44954.09點，再創歷史新高。然而，由於短線漲幅過大，加上市場傳出美伊戰事再度升溫，引發國際股市避險情緒，台股隨後由紅翻黑，盤中最低下探43378.24點，單日高低震盪高達1575點，「44K」關卡失守。\n

\n

權值股走跌，台積電一度衝上2360元天價，目前翻黑跌10元至2290元。台達電及聯發科分別跌4.56%及6.25%，聯電攻151元新高價後下殺，跌逾2.5%。\n

觀察今日盤勢，台股在開盤後買氣強勁，早上9點23分時，指數大漲697.29點，來到44954.09點，市場原本瀰漫樂觀氛圍。不過，隨著指數持續衝高，市場獲利了結賣壓逐步湧現，盤勢開始震盪走弱。\n

此外，國際情勢變化也成為拖累盤面的關鍵因素。市場傳出美國與伊朗之間戰事再起，導致亞洲主要股市同步走跌，投資人避險情緒快速升溫，台股也受到影響，賣壓明顯加重。到了上午11點40分左右，指數更急殺至43378.24點，較盤中高點大幅回落，一度重挫878.56點。\n

法人指出，近期台股漲勢凌厲，指數頻創新高，市場累積不少獲利籌碼，因此只要遇到國際利空消息，盤面波動就容易明顯放大。尤其地緣政治風險升溫，更加劇投資人觀望氣氛，使得資金轉趨保守。\n

news.Title: Nvidia Stock Has Struggled Over the Last Week. Here's What I Think Is Going On.
news.Content:
Shares of artificial intelligence (AI) chipmaker Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) have struggled lately. The stock closed at about $213 on Wednesday, down about 6% over the past week and off about 10% from an all-time high of $236.54 earlier this month.

That weakness comes despite a fiscal first-quarter report on May 20 that, by almost any measure, was outstanding. Revenue rose 85% year over year to a record $81.6 billion. Data center revenue climbed 92% to $75.2 billion. And management raised the quarterly dividend from $0.01 per share to $0.25 per share while authorizing an additional $80 billion in share repurchases.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

So, what is going on?

The quarter itself was exceptional

Nvidia's fiscal first quarter of 2027 (the period ended April 26, 2026) really was something.

The headline 85% revenue growth marked a sharp acceleration from 73% growth in fiscal Q4 -- and the chipmaker's profit trajectory was equally striking. Non-GAAP (adjusted) earnings per share of $1.87 rose 140% year over year, and free cash flow climbed to $49 billion in the quarter alone.

And underpinning this business momentum was relentless data center demand, with hyperscalers accounting for roughly half of data center revenue -- and the rest from a widening mix of AI cloud and enterprise customers, alongside a fast-growing sovereign AI vertical. Data center networking revenue alone nearly tripled year over year to about $15 billion.

Even more, management expects even stronger growth ahead. Nvidia guided to fiscal Q2 revenue of $91 billion, plus or minus 2%. This implies about 95% year-over-year growth.

"Demand has gone parabolic," said Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang during the company's fiscal first-quarter earnings call. "The reason is simple. Agentic AI has arrived. AI can now do productive and valuable work."

So what could investors possibly be worried about?

What may be weighing on shares

For starters, the stock was arguably priced near perfection heading into the report. Going into earnings, shares had climbed about 20% off their February low, hitting an all-time high less than a week before the report.

But there are also a couple of real concerns surfacing in the filings themselves. One has to do with how concentrated Nvidia's customer base has become. The chipmaker's top three customers accounted for about 64% of accounts receivable in the latest quarter -- up from roughly 56% the prior quarter. That kind of concentration could leave Nvidia exposed if even one or two of those buyers were to pull back.

Story Continues

Another has to do with how much capital Nvidia is locking up to meet anticipated demand. The company's inventory and total supply related commitments swelled to about $145 billion as of the end of the quarter. That is a lot of capital tied to forward demand assumptions in an industry that has historically gone through cycles.

And it doesn't help that data center product shipments to mainland China remain essentially on pause. Nvidia recorded no data center compute revenue from China during the quarter, compared to $4.6 billion in the year-ago period.

Despite these issues, the underlying business appears to be firing on all cylinders. The breadth of demand and the size of the new capital return commitments together suggest management has plenty of confidence in the path ahead. And it is worth remembering that this is now the fourth straight quarter the stock has slipped after a beat-and-raise report -- a pattern that may say more about how much optimism the market keeps trying to price in than about any single quarter's results.

For now, I think investors who already own the stock should remain patient. Shares could keep wobbling as the market digests both the explosive growth and the growing risks. But the underlying business does not appear to be breaking down. It just may need some time to grow into the expectations placed on it.

news.Title: 跳水了？台股狂殺700點 上下震盪1500點
news.Content:
台股今（28）日早盤開高震盪，盤中一度上漲600點來到44954點創新高，怎料盤中突然跳水，狂殺700點，上下震盪逾1500點。連帶台積電翻黑下跌10元，聯發科則重挫7%，來到4305元。

根據《CBNC》報導，美國對伊朗發動新的攻擊，而伊朗半官方的塔斯尼姆通訊社指出，伊朗革命衛隊週四表示，他們在當地時間凌晨4點50分左右襲擊了一處美國空軍基地，革命衛隊沒有具體說明該空軍基地的位置。

由於美伊新衝突，市場擔心荷莫茲海峽的商業航運會受到干擾，國際油價因此上漲3%，來到至每桶 97.29美元，連帶影響亞洲股市下挫，日經指數於平盤附近震盪，韓股則下跌逾1%。

分析師股添樂（陳相州）在臉書粉專表示，「大家可以專心上班了」今天盤中上下震盪超過1500點，看來是有明顯獲利賣壓出籠，他也指出，有漲有跌的股市，才是正常健康的。

（封面圖／東森新聞）

news.Title: 【台股盤中】放量震盪守44500點　53千金曇花一現
news.Content:
（中央社記者鍾榮峰台北2026年5月28日電）美股三大指數同步創高，台積電ADR和期貨夜盤再寫新猷，亞股走強，台股今天早盤強勢走高，盤中觸44954.09點歷史新高，上漲697.29點，之後震盪漲點收斂，上下波動超過710點，台積電觸2360元新高價後回檔，聯發科、台達電等電子權值股翻黑。

到11時10分，加權指數44576.47點，上漲319.67點，成交值放大至約新台幣8657億元，電子指數盤中漲幅收斂至0.75%，金融指數翻紅勁揚0.32%，代表中小型股的櫃買OTC指數盤中漲幅收斂至0.85%。

權王台積電 (2330) 早盤最高觸2360元，再創歷史新高價，漲60元，漲幅2.6%，市值攻上新台幣61.2兆元，續創新大量，盤中漲點收斂至2330元，漲30元。其他電子權值股台達電 (2308) 盤中翻黑跌約2%，聯發科 (2454) 跌2.5%，鴻海 (2317) 漲幅收斂，日月光投控 (3711) 震盪小漲，聯電 (2303) 漲1.7%。

記憶體族群南亞科 (2408) 勁揚7.8%、華邦電 (2344) 小跌，旺宏 (2337) 大漲逾7%。

股價超過千元的高價股漲跌互見，環球晶 (6488) 一度大漲9%躋身千元行列，之後得而復失，台股「53千金」曇花一現。

台新台灣AI優息動能ETF基金研究團隊指出，美伊衝突走向和談，系統性風險降低，市場風險偏好回升，加上美股財報季，科技股在AI帶動下持續繳出亮眼成績，台積電展望正向加速擴產，資金再度擁抱AI大趨勢，帶動台股加權指數連日飆漲，突破44000點大關。

展望台股後市，台新台灣AI優息動能ETF基金研究團隊指出，近期內部風險需留意融資持續攀升，籌碼面漸趨凌亂，外部環境主要風險在於通膨較預期走高，不過持續正面看待AI行情。

news.Title: SpaceX Could Be Worth More Than Tesla the Moment It Goes Public. Here's Why.
news.Content:
SpaceX is gearing up for what could be the largest initial public offering (IPO) in history. The rocket and satellite specialist publicly filed its S-1 with the Securities and Exchange Commission last week, and reports point to a Nasdaq debut under the ticker SPCX as soon as June 12. Pricing is expected the night before, with the investor roadshow kicking off in early June.

The targeted valuation: somewhere between $1.75 trillion and $2 trillion. That range is notable because it would put SpaceX above Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) -- the electric vehicle and energy company run by SpaceX CEO Elon Musk -- on day one. Tesla's market capitalization sits at about $1.65 trillion as of this writing.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on a little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly," providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need.

Continue »

So how does a company that lost about $4.9 billion last year leapfrog an automaker generating more than $22 billion in quarterly revenue? The answer has less to do with rockets than with what the rockets put into orbit.

Starlink is doing the heavy lifting

For all the attention focused on Starship, the part of SpaceX doing the financial heavy lifting is Starlink. The connectivity segment, which is primarily Starlink, generated $11.4 billion in revenue in 2025 -- up nearly 50% year over year, according to the S-1. Operating income for the segment more than doubled to $4.4 billion, and adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA) jumped 86% to roughly $7.2 billion.

Subscriber growth is similarly impressive. Today, Starlink boasts 10.3 million subscribers across 164 countries, up from 8.9 million at the end of 2025, 4.4 million in 2024, and 2.3 million in 2023.

Revenue per subscriber, however, has come down -- from about $99 a month in 2023 to roughly $66 in March -- as cheaper plans and expansion into developing markets pull the average down. Still, the segment generated $3.3 billion in revenue in the first quarter alone, with $2.1 billion of adjusted EBITDA. Starlink, on its own, is now a major satellite business growing at an uncanny rate.

The headline net loss largely reflects something else. SpaceX's newly consolidated artificial intelligence (AI) segment, which folds in xAI following a February merger that valued the combined entity at $1.25 trillion, lost $6.4 billion in 2025 and another $2.5 billion in the first quarter. SpaceX said in the S-1 that it expects to begin deploying orbital AI compute satellites "as early as 2028."

Story Continues

Tesla, meanwhile, is in transition

The contrast with Tesla is what makes the comparison interesting. The electric vehicle and energy company just emerged from its first year of annual revenue decline, with 2025 sales falling for the first time in its history as a public company. First-quarter results in 2026 showed revenue rising 16% to $22.4 billion, but deliveries of 358,023 missed expectations, and management told investors that capital expenditures would climb above $25 billion this year, largely to fund AI compute and upcoming products like the Cybercab.

So while Tesla is plowing record sums into projects that won't generate meaningful revenue until 2027 or later, the market is being asked to size up a SpaceX whose AI bets are similarly speculative but whose anchor business -- satellite broadband -- is already booming.

Notably, Musk cautioned during Tesla's first-quarter earnings call that the company's autonomous ride-sharing business won't be material this year. Strip that story out, and what's left is an automaker with a vehicle business that contracted in 2025 and an energy segment that posted a 12% revenue decline in the first quarter -- a dismal business for one trading at a price-to-earnings ratio of about 400 as of this writing.

Ultimately, this is a useful reminder that the market is already paying a heavy multiple for stories Tesla hasn't yet shipped at scale.

That same patience -- the willingness to pay up today for businesses that may take years to deliver -- is what SpaceX is asking the public to extend the moment it lists.

We just issued 'double down' alerts on 3 stocks — find out if Tesla made our list

Ever feel like you missed the boat in buying the most successful stocks? Then you’ll want to hear this.

On rare occasions, our expert team of analysts issues a “Double Down” stock recommendation for companies that they think are about to pop. If you’re worried you’ve already missed your chance to invest, now is the best time to buy before it’s too late. And the numbers speak for themselves:

Nvidia: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2009, you’d have $557,318 !*

Apple: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2008, you’d have $58,766 !*

Netflix: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2004, you’d have $472,852 !*

Right now, we’re issuing “Double Down” alerts for three incredible companies , available when you join Stock Advisor , and there may not be another chance like this anytime soon.

See the 3 stocks »

*Stock Advisor returns as of May 26, 2026

Daniel Sparks has clients with positions in Tesla. The Motley Fool has positions in and recommends Tesla. The Motley Fool has a disclosure policy .

news.Title: 台積電創高撐盤，中小型電子股續強
news.Content:
【財訊快報／黃冠豪】亞股在前一日AI與半導體股大漲後進入高檔整理。日經225指數早盤維持平盤震盪，追價動能明顯降溫；韓國KOSPI也回檔至8,178點附近，下跌約1%，顯示前一日韓股大漲後，資金短線轉向獲利了結。韓國大型記憶體股表現分歧，三星電子（005930.KS）盤中報30萬韓元，下跌2%；SK海力士（000660.KS）則逆勢上漲0.86%，報226.2萬韓元，反映市場仍偏愛HBM與AI記憶體供應鏈。台股則相對強勢，加權指數盤中報44,560點，上漲300點，漲幅0.68%，早盤最高來到44,954.09點，再度改寫高點紀錄；上櫃指數同步走揚，盤中來到444點，漲幅0.9%，中小型股人氣仍明顯優於大型權值股。

權值股方面，台積電(2330)仍是撐盤核心，盤中報2,335元，上漲35元，最高觸及2,360元，續創波段新高，成為指數能夠維持紅盤的關鍵。聯發科(2454)則在前波急漲後震盪，盤中報4,515元，下跌125元，跌幅2.5%；台達電(2308)同樣拉回，盤中跌破2,500元，下跌50元，跌幅1.8%。聯電(2303)則相對持穩，盤中登上151元，續創新高。

盤面強勢族群仍集中在電子零組件與漲價題材。被動元件延續資金熱度，國巨*(2327)、華新科(2492)、凱美(2375)等指標股持續受到關注，主因是AI伺服器、車用與工控需求回溫，帶動MLCC、電阻等報價想像。石英元件族群表現更為亮眼，晶技(3042)、台嘉碩(3221)盤中一度強攻，加高(8182)、希華(2484)、安碁(6174)也有資金點火，市場解讀AI伺服器、5G與HPC需求推升高頻、低功耗元件需求。記憶體族群則延續國際記憶體缺貨與報價上漲題材，南亞科(2408)、旺宏(2337)、華邦電(2344)、威剛(3260)、群聯(8299)仍是盤中人氣焦點，美光大好，力積電(6770)強攻漲停，是記憶體族群中的亮點。

整體來看，今日台股呈現「台積電撐指數、中小型題材股續強」格局，不過聯發科、台達電等高價權值股已出現震盪，代表短線漲多後市場開始挑股操作。若午後台積電續穩、上櫃指數維持強勢，盤面仍有利被動元件、石英元件與記憶體族群輪動；但指數逼近4萬5關卡，追高仍需留意獲利了結賣壓。

news.Title: 世界先進：下半年景氣應優於上半年　產能供不應求
news.Content:
（中央社記者張建中新竹2026年5月28日電）晶圓代工廠世界先進 (5347) 董事長方略今天表示，下半年景氣應可優於上半年，產能供不應求；至於代工價格方面，世界先進會隨時觀察市場變化、競爭態勢和客戶需求，持開放態度。

世界先進今天召開股東常會，方略會後受訪說，觀察人工智慧（AI）需求及發展趨勢，預期下半年產業景氣應可優於上半年。

針對代工價格，方略表示，世界先進一直與客戶合作，如果成本不斷增加或不斷投資，產業鏈也支持調整價格，會與客戶重新檢視價格有多少調整空間。

方略說，目前水、電、材料都在漲，人力和產能需求增加，世界先進會小心應對，會隨時觀察市場變化、競爭態勢和客戶需求，對於價格調整持開放態度。

至於客戶下單情況，方略表示，現在產能都滿，供不應求，客戶知道這樣的情況，不過為確保產能，客戶仍積極下單。（編輯：張良知）

news.Title: Why IREN Stock Popped Today
news.Content:
Shares of IREN Limited (NASDAQ: IREN) climbed on Wednesday after the artificial intelligence (AI) data center developer struck a deal with Dell Technologies (NYSE: DELL) .

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

A need for speed

IREN agreed to purchase Blackwell systems from Dell featuring Nvidia 's high-performance graphics processing units ( GPUs ). The $1.6 billion purchase price includes Nvidia's GPUs, Dell's servers, and related storage, networking, and ancillary equipment.

IREN plans to deploy these systems in its existing data centers at its Childress, Texas campus. It expects to bring them online by early 2027.

"Our relationship with Dell ensures access to hardware at the scale and speed the market demands," IREN Co-CEO Daniel Roberts said. "Every deployment we complete makes the next one faster, and that compounding execution advantage is what we are building."

Pressing its advantage

It's this rapid deployment speed that gives IREN an edge over its competitors. Other projects launching now are unlikely to bring their data centers fully online before the end of the decade.

"If you wanted to start today and build a gigawatt AI factory, you are looking at the 2030s before you get the first compute online," Roberts said during an interview with Bloomberg earlier this month.

Investors should expect IREN to continue to invest aggressively in its computing resources to hasten its expansion schedule.

"Securing capacity and accelerating commissioning are our top priorities in a market where time-to-compute is everything," Roberts said.

news.Title: Sandisk Has Climbed From $36 to $1,590 in a Year. What the AI Memory Boom Means for the Stock From Here.
news.Content:
Up more than 4,000% over the past year and about 570% so far in 2026, Sandisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) stock has been one of the most extraordinary winners in the artificial intelligence (AI) build-out. The NAND flash memory specialist, which only began trading on its own in February 2025 after being spun off from Western Digital , has climbed from a 52-week low of about $36 to a closing price near $1,590 as of this writing.

With shares at an all-time high and a market value above $230 billion, has the easy money been made?

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on a little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly," providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need.

Continue »

Maybe. But the more interesting question is whether the underlying business has actually changed enough to justify thinking about this as something more than a typical memory-cycle peak.

An AI-led shift in the business

The headline numbers from Sandisk's fiscal third quarter (the period ended April 3, 2026) were staggering. Revenue jumped 251% year over year to $5.95 billion, up 97% from the prior quarter alone.

Profitability moved even faster. Sandisk's non-GAAP (adjusted) gross margin reached 78.4%, up from 51.1% in fiscal Q2 and just 22.5% in the year-ago period. And the company's adjusted earnings per share came in at $23.41 -- well above management's own forecast of $12 to $14.

Driving this growth, data center revenue hit $1.47 billion in fiscal Q3, up 233% sequentially and roughly 645% from the year-ago period. Capturing the staggering sequential momentum of the segment, three months earlier that same segment had grown about 64% sequentially.

The acceleration reflects a deliberate redirection of the company's bit supply toward higher-value AI infrastructure customers.

Management is now formalizing that redirection through what it calls New Business Models (NBMs) -- multi-year supply contracts backed by firm financial guarantees. Sandisk ended fiscal Q3 with three signed NBMs and has added two more since, bringing the total to five. The three signed during the quarter alone carry about $42 billion in remaining performance obligations (RPO), and the five together include more than $11 billion in financial guarantees. Together, they cover more than a third of the company's bit supply for fiscal 2027.

CEO David Goeckeler explained why customers are willing to commit.

"We run a fab. We have very consistent output. We need very consistent consumption," he said on the company's fiscal third-quarter earnings call.

Story Continues

In short, customers get close to what is "locked-in" supply -- and Sandisk gets unusual visibility into demand, with real money on the line if a customer walks.

There's also a product catalyst still in front of investors. Management plans to begin shipping QLC Stargate solid-state drives -- designed for high-density AI workloads -- for revenue this fiscal fourth quarter.

What's already priced in

But there's a lot priced in.

Sandisk's price-to-earnings ratio now sits above 50. Of course, the company's trailing earnings aren't very representative of its earnings power today. But even its forward price-to-earnings ratio in the mid-twenties represents a valuation that prices in strong earnings growth for years to come.

NAND has historically been one of the most cyclical segments of the semiconductor industry. And Goeckeler himself has acknowledged that long-term memory contracts have unraveled before during downturns. The NBMs are designed to make this cycle different, but they're also new and include variable pricing components, meaning a meaningful drop in spot pricing could still flow through to results.

Competition is another consideration. A faster-than-expected supply response from rivals like Samsung or SK Hynix , or any softening in cloud-provider capital spending -- would likely weigh on the business and the stock.

So yes, Sandisk is structurally a different company than it was a year ago, and its soaring stock price makes sense in hindsight. The contracts, the data center mix, the cash generation, and the recently authorized $6 billion share repurchase program all support that idea. But at this price, an investor is essentially paying for the new Sandisk while still inheriting the risks of the old one, namely, cyclicality. With all of this said, for those who want exposure to the AI memory build-out from here, keeping any position relatively small could make sense.

news.Title: 分紅爭議燒不完？ 他發文直諫魏哲家 員工最不滿「這件事」
news.Content:
「護國神山」台積電（TSMC）近日因傳出調降員工分紅15%，引發員工強烈不滿。對此，公司董事長魏哲家特別取消前往荷蘭的行程，親自與員工舉辦QA座談會。魏哲家坦言，今年分紅總額將比去年增加三成，並強調「台積電分紅沒有天花板」，對公司未來依然充滿信心。不過，這場分紅風波卻未因此平息，獨立媒體Culpium創辦人高燦鳴（Tim Culpan）直言，這正是台積電當前「有史以來最大的危機」。

魏哲家表示，面對員工最在意的薪資及福利，魏哲家在座談會中細列四大方針，包括調整分紅分配、年度調薪優先照顧基層、確保不裁員，以及公司利潤部分將投入ESG和公益事業。不過，儘管目前尚未有結構性調薪計畫，但年度調薪會以低職級員工為主，即使對半導體業界激烈的人才爭奪戰，魏哲家認為台積電有足夠豐厚的待遇可以吸引人才加入。

同時，魏哲家也對台積電的前景非常有信心，強烈鼓勵員工將分紅轉為投資、買進自家的股票。他直言，「積極拿分紅投資台積電，絕對能保證未來的生活無虞。」

對此，高燦鳴表示，雖然魏哲家親自與員工QA座談，但僅限公司內部員工參加，透過網路直播到公司禮堂及會議室，並在10分鐘內就被預訂一空。傳言錯過預定時段的員工無法加入，這反映了公司對資訊外洩的極度擔憂。

高燦鳴指出，儘管高層極力安撫，但許多員工最憤慨的是，魏哲家去年的總薪酬飆升至7800萬美元，折合新台幣24億元（約新台幣24億元）遠高於多數員工預期。高燦鳴說，「我採訪的一些員工在得知CEO的總薪酬後都驚呆了，這筆巨款中只有一小部分是他的薪水，大部分來自與業績掛鉤的獎金、津貼以及利潤分成。但除了實際數字之外，魏哲家的薪資佔淨利的比例在2025年增加了75%，他的薪酬增長速度也超過了員工薪酬的中位數。」

高燦鳴分析，西方國家對於高階主管的高額薪水司空見慣，但台灣員工普遍希望管理階層與普通員工的薪酬維持一定的平等，此外，魏哲家則在會中避而不談關於高層薪酬細節，並強調薪酬方案由董事會決定、淡化成立類似三星員工工會的可能性，暗示這種工會對員工「弊大於利」。

高燦鳴表示，據消息人士透露，魏哲家並未在會議中對台積電修改分紅方案的決定做出讓步。他認為，「這次事件或許暫時安撫了一場叛亂，但三星的教訓表明，管理層不應將員工的善意視為理所當然。」

高燦鳴強調，本次事件更凸顯公司治理與員工關係的脆弱，若怠於妥善處理，恐將衍生人才流失和內部信任裂痕，台積電薪資結構、分紅制度與溝通機制正面臨嚴峻考驗，恐成為台積電未來全球競爭的最大隱憂。

最後，高燦鳴直言，魏哲家由基層做起，一定知道員工是台積電能夠擊敗英特爾、三星、聯電等眾多競爭對手的根本原因。也正是員工，才使得蘋果、輝達、AMD 等數百家公司對這家位於新竹的科技巨頭始終保持著高度的忠誠度，「將員工的善意視為理所當然，最終只會引爆更大的危機。」目前留給台積電管理層的，除了安撫人心、完善制度，更重要的是重建員工信任。

（封面圖／東森新聞）

news.Title: 聯發科這局贏很大？ 郭明錤揭秘：馬斯克宇宙算力的關鍵拼圖
news.Content:
聯發科有望成為馬斯克Terafab關鍵拼圖，郭明錤揭秘其將全面支援Intel 14A先進製程，雙方計畫於2028年小量生產晶片。憑藉與Intel合作經驗、Google TPU實績及台灣「夜鷹」研發生態系，聯發科將助Terafab突破極限。

馬斯克晶片夢的多重壓力

天風國際證券分析師郭明錤在臉書發文表示，馬斯克的半導體願景雖然有著激勵人心的長期敘事，但其專屬的IC設計團隊正面臨極大的執行壓力。這包括必須同時與台積電、Samsung與Intel三方合作的廣大範疇，以及開發宇宙環境優化晶片的技術門檻。此外，Terafab面臨產業常態一半以下的九個月極短設計週期，且人力規模粗估僅有Apple SEG團隊的數分之一，卻要在更緊迫的時間內執行更廣的營運目標。

聯發科成Terafab最佳合作對象

根據郭明錤分析，在數家客製化ASIC廠商中，聯發科較有可能成為Terafab的策略合作對象。聯發科不僅具有投片Intel 16與參與先進封裝EMIB-T的實際經驗，更因與Google合作TPU 8t及Humufish進度超預期，展現出具備Tier-1量級的量產能力。郭明錤指出：「聯發科將全面支援Intel 14A先進製程與先進封裝的導入與生產，預計自2028年開始小量生產Musk的IC設計團隊所需晶片。」

台灣生態系助攻加速研發

除了公司本身的技術實力，聯發科背後還有台灣獨有的半導體生態系加持。郭明錤分析，台積電24/7輪班研發的「夜鷹計畫」是提升執行效率的關鍵，而聯發科能將此加速模式導入Terafab。目前已有美國上游材料商在台灣設立實驗室仿效此模式，成效令Apple滿意。若Terafab與聯發科合作，藉由美台兩地跨時區換手研發，將能有效強化執行效率，確保在2026年10月取得Intel 14A PDK 0.9後，能第一時間搶佔2028年的生產窗口。

聯發科今（28）日在挑戰前高4690元未果後，股價小幅拉回，一度回測4500點整數關卡，目前暫報4555元，下跌90元或1.94%。

（封面示意圖/AI生成）

news.Title: 台灣不只台積電！專家點名2檔「新神山」：鴻海看不到車尾燈
news.Content:
台股持續強勢上攻，今（28）日早盤大漲逾600點，最高來到44954.09點，整體市值已逼近5兆美元，正式超越印度，躍升全球第五大股市。財信傳媒董事長謝金河在臉書發文表示，台灣除了台積電有台積電這座護國神山，聯發科、台達電也是兩座世界級的大山。

謝金河指出，隨著全球科技供應鏈重組，以及人工智慧（AI）產業快速崛起，台灣在半導體與資通訊產業的優勢更加明顯。他認為，今年台灣經濟成長率甚至有機會挑戰雙位數成長，在出口表現帶動下，台灣也有望擠進全球前十大出口國。

對於過去外界常以「一座神山」形容台灣產業結構，謝金河認為，台灣如今已逐漸形成「群山效應」，除了台積電之外，聯發科與台達電已成為兩座世界級企業代表。

其中，謝金河提到聯發科，該公司近年積極轉型，已不再只是手機晶片廠，而是進一步切入AI ASIC供應鏈，並逐步挑戰國際大廠博通（Broadcom）等企業地位。目前聯發科市值已逾7.4兆元新台幣，約2366億美元，不僅逼近高通，若與大陸大型企業相比，也已超越工業富聯與中國移動等公司。

另一家受到關注的企業則是台達電，謝金河表示，台達電已成為全球高壓直流輸電（HVDC）領域的重要企業，台達電最早超車鴻海，目前市值逾6.5兆元新台幣，鴻海已經看不到台達電的車尾燈。

台達電已晉升2000億美元級國際企業，旗下子公司泰達電在泰國市場表現同樣強勢，目前股價已來到354泰銖，市值達4.416兆泰銖，帶動台灣在泰國的企業同步走揚，最近泰金寶股價大漲，今年的漲幅接近100%。

謝金河進一步表示，除了科技龍頭表現亮眼之外，近期台股也呈現多點開花格局，包括聯電市值突破1.8兆元，國巨市值攀升至1.45兆元，董事長陳泰銘更因此登上台灣首富寶座。他文末總結，台灣科技產業正逐步形成完整且具國際競爭力的產業群聚。

news.Title: Tesla's Optimus robot plan hits major snag
news.Content:
Tesla CEO Elon Musk let investors know early on that 2026 would be an expensive year for the company.

Coming off of its first-ever decline in revenue and its second consecutive year of falling deliveries in 2025, 2026 was always going to be a pivotal year for the electric vehicle maker.

Musk already says Tesla ( TSLA ) is much more than a car company, so when the company raised its capital expenditure forecast to over $25 billion for mostly non-car-related development, most analysts didn't blink ( though some did ).

A significant portion of that money is going to retooling the company's car production capacity to make room for the production of 1 million Optimus humanoid robots annually.

While Musk has been extremely optimistic about Optimus, calling it perhaps "the most important product" in Tesla's history , there seems to be one major problem with his grand vision for the future: not many people are looking to buy a $30,000 humanoid robot.

The capital the company plans to spend on transforming Model S and Model X manufacturing capacity into Optimus manufacturing capacity won't have much return until there is consumer demand for the product.

"We are replacing the S and X line in Fremont with a million units per year line of Optimus. Because it is a completely new supply chain , there's really nothing from the existing supply chains that exist in Optimus," Musk said during the first quarter earnings call .

"Frankly, if we can halt production of a line, dismantle the entire production line, reinstall a completely new one, and get it up and running within four months, that would be an incredibly fast pace... This process will take at least several months. You then need to install new production lines, provide all necessary wiring and communication, and test the machines on them. This also takes several months,” Musk said.

But while the Model S and Model X have been sales laggards for years , Tesla doesn't really have a plan to replace the revenue from the tens of thousands of vehicles it sold last year.

Tesla Optimus plans are missing one key piece: demand

Lack of demand led Tesla to mothball the Model X and Model S, but the company is betting billions that demand for humanoid robots will be there to replace them. But there is scant evidence for this train of thought.

"Tesla’s humanoid robots have hardly demonstrated any notable capabilities, but they’re supposed to cost tens of thousands of dollars each once they’re launched," Zachary Shahan of CleanTechnica wrote recently .

Part of the issue is that Optimus robots are extremely expensive. The robot costs $10,000 to produce at Giga Texas, according to Tesla Car World , and could retail for between $20,000 and $30,000, according to StandardBots .

Story Continues

"Normal people can’t plop down $30,000 on a robot companion, and how many super-rich people are really going to benefit from one? And $30,000 is probably an overly optimistic price. Who is going to spend $30,000 or more on what is basically a toy?" Shahan said.

Shahan points out that, despite the billions now being spent on the project, Optimus won't be for sale until the end of 2027 at the earliest.

"Maybe I’ve missed something on how this makes much more sense than keeping the Model S and Model X production lines running," Shahan concluded.

Tesla's auto business is its biggest money maker despite visions of a robotic future

Coming off a year where the company posted its first-ever annual revenue loss, 2026 was always going to be pivotal for Tesla. While CEO Elon Musk told investors that the company would focus on autonomy and humanoid robots this year, car sales account for 73% of Tesla’s revenue .

When combined with its leasing department, automotive accounts for 85%-87% of Tesla’s revenue.

Meanwhile, most of Elon Musk's comments on its $25 billion capex plan (up from its previous guidance of $20 billion) focused on Optimus and autonomous driving.

However, Optimus sales are still 18 months away (best-case scenario), and Tesla’s overall Full Self-Driving (Supervised) adoption rate is climbing , but is still only about 14% with 1.28 million active subscribers.

More Tesla news

Tesla makes a necessary change, fans are going to hate

Tesla plans Mag 7 spending spree analysts call 'difficult to justify'

Tesla CEO Elon Musk 'injects some realism' into Q1 earnings call

To bolster its biggest moneymaker, Tesla is raising the price of its popular Model Y in the U.S., Reuters reported . The Model Y premium all-wheel drive and Model Y premium rear-wheel drive had their prices increased by $1,000 each to $49,990 and $45,990, respectively. Tesla also raised the price of the Model Y Performance all-wheel drive by $500 to $57,990.

This is the second time in two years that Tesla has raised the price of the Model Y, following a $1,000 increase in 2024.

But Tesla's pivot to non-car revenue also makes a ton of sense.

Auto sales already aren’t a high-margin business, and its automotive gross margin (excluding regulatory credits) actually dipped into the red for the first time in 2025, according to Reuters .

Related: Tesla makes necessary change fans are going to hate

This story was originally published by TheStreet on May 27, 2026, where it first appeared in the Technology section. Add TheStreet as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

news.Title: 黃仁勳兆元宴今登場！20檔概念股開紅花 光寶科台達電4檔逆勢走弱
news.Content:
輝達執行長黃仁勳今（28）日傍晚將舉行「兆元宴」，預計包含台積電魏哲家、廣達林百里、鴻海劉揚偉等科技大廠董總都將成為座上賓。《Yahoo股市》統計24檔「兆元宴概念股」有20檔開紅花，僅台達電（2308）、聯發科（2454）、智邦（2345）、光寶科（2301）4檔逆勢走弱。分析師表示，這4檔個股皆為漲多後獲利了結賣壓湧現，出現震盪整理屬正常現象。

此次兆元宴延續過往「老地方、老班底」的傳統，選在黃仁勳偏愛的「磚窯古早味餐廳」舉行，這也是他第五度在同一地點宴請供應鏈夥伴，這場餐敘將成為市場觀察輝達AI戰略推進與供應鏈布局的關鍵風向球。

《Yahoo股市》統計24檔「兆元宴概念股」涵蓋晶圓製造、伺服器代工、PC、散熱、網通、IC設計、半導體封測等多元領域，分別有欣興（3037）、台達電（2308）、宜鼎（5289）、聯發科（2454）、台積電（2330）、京元電（2449）、智邦（2345）、緯穎（6669）、光寶科（2301）、奇鋐（3017）、鴻海（2317）、研華（2395）、華碩（2357）、技嘉（2376）、緯創（3231）、仁寶（2324）、廣達（2382）、英業達（2356）、華擎（3515）、永擎（7711）、宏碁（2353）、和碩（4938）、微星（2377）、廣運（6125）。

其中，智邦今日盤中重挫逾5%，光寶科下跌超過4%，台達電、聯發科亦有超過2%跌幅。資深證券分析師王兆立表示，4檔個股沒有特別利空，而是過去一段時間累計漲幅已相當可觀，因此在市場短線資金調節下，出現震盪整理屬正常現象。

其中，台達電乖離率偏大，目前追價風險相對偏高，若投資人想進一步布局，建議等待股價拉回月線約2180-2200元附近再分批承接；對於已經持有相關概念股的投資人，王兆立則建議，若手中已有獲利，可考慮先行部分減碼、落袋為安，等待後續整理後再觀察新的進場機會。

馮萱榕／財經特派記者，主跑財政部、電腦通路、觀光餐飲等

news.Title: 聯發科參展Computex 秀資料中心解決方案/光通訊技術
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-05-28 09:37:25 萬惠雯 發佈

聯發科(2454)將以AI Without Limits為主題，參展Computex 2026，展出在AI世代下從邊緣到雲端的次世代技術與解決方案。總經理陳冠州表示，聯發科在Agentic AI趨勢下，從邊緣端至雲端都擁有絕佳優勢，不僅為各類邊緣裝置提供極致算力、更布局雲端資料中心技術、也持續推進無縫串聯邊緣與雲端的通訊技術。在產業邁向AI大趨勢的關鍵轉折點上，聯發科技將持續攜手全球夥伴、客戶、AI生態系及半導體供應鏈，加速實現無所不在的AI，並進一步擴大AI基礎設施投資。

聯發科與NVIDIA在此次展會中，將共同展出搭載NVIDIA GB10 Grace Blackwell超級晶片的Agentic AI超級電腦NVIDIA DGX Spark，具備1 petaFLOP效能GPU、20核心CPU、LPDDR5x統一架構記憶體，能在裝置上直接運作大型AI模型，以自動且智慧協調執行任務，並重新定義桌上型裝置的智慧生產力。此外，也於展會領先展出搭載NVIDIA G-SYNC Pulsar技術之顯示控制晶片（Scaler）之電競螢幕。

另外，聯發科表示，聯發科資料中心解決方案不僅提供單一零組件，更涵蓋端到端的AI資料中心平台。從客製化ASIC與XPU設計、2.5D/3.5D先進封裝技術、頂尖的高速互連技術，到機櫃層級整合，將AI擴展的底層架構創新整合至單一系統，協助客戶在大規模部署AI資料中心的同時，達到卓越的總體擁有成本（TCO）效能比與每瓦效能表現。

另外，聯發科因應產業朝導入矽光子以提升頻寬密度的發展方向，展會上將展出高達400Gbps/fiber(單根光纖)頻寬速率之共同封裝光學（CPO）技術，以及可應用於資料中心互連主動式光纜（AOC）之MicroLED光學技術應用，可單晶整合至與現有資料中心設備相容的CMOS收發器中，擁有銅線的可靠度並降低50%功耗。其中MicroLED光學技術可延伸應用至CPO與NPO技術。

在6G部分，聯發科將展出兩項最新6G技術概念。作為6G標準制定的積極推動者，聯發科展出全球首次的「6G無線接取互通性（Radio Interoperability）」成果，在兼顧高速傳輸的同時達到低網路延遲與低功耗的優異調度彈性，成為支援未來，達成混合邊緣與雲端的Agentic AI協作模式，適用於機器人之AI對AI、人類對AI、服務對AI的大規模Agentic AI應用情境。另一為6G裝置協作多天線（Device Collaborative MIMO，Co-MIMO）技術，讓手機或穿戴裝置聚合室內其他6G裝置之訊號，有效增加下行吞吐量六成以上，為低延遲應用情境加持。

盛群切入伺服器散熱風扇與光通訊，最快明年放量

AI題材夯 邁威爾再刷新高、分析師接力唱多

資料來源- MoneyDJ理財網

news.Title: Barclays resets SanDisk stock price target after earnings
news.Content:
Sandisk was trading near $36 twelve months ago. It has since done something that most stocks never do in a lifetime. And on May 27, one of Wall Street 's major banks looked at that run and decided the story was not finished.

The argument it made for why is more specific than most investors are expecting.

What Barclays changed and the analyst behind the call

Barclays analyst Tom O'Malley upgraded SanDisk (SNDK) to Overweight from Equal-weight and raised his price target to $2,300 from $1,200 on May 27. The new target implies approximately 45% upside from the stock's close of $1,589.55. Shares jumped 7.5% on the day of the upgrade.

More Wall Street:

JPMorgan resets S&P 500 price target for the rest of 2026

Vanguard challenges the S&P 500 as a one-stop strategy

Goldman Sachs resets Broadcom stock forecast

The 52-week low for SanDisk was $36.21. The stock has gained more than 4,000% in the past twelve months, according to Investing.com . Barclays is saying the move is not over.

Why O'Malley's thesis centers on contracts, not just pricing

The draft narrative around SanDisk upgrades has focused on memory pricing and supply cycles. O'Malley's argument is more specific and more structural than that. His core thesis is about SanDisk's contracting model, not just the market cycle it operates in.

O'Malley said SanDisk has taken an "aggressive and structurally innovative" approach to its New Business Models, highlighted at the company's most recent earnings call . The NBMs are designed to deliver "demand certainty" for SanDisk while giving customers "supply assurance," according to TipRanks .

The contracts vary in length, with the longest extending into 2031. They are structured around quarterly volume commitments that scale upward over the life of each agreement. Pricing combines fixed rates in the near term with variable pricing over the longer horizon, allowing SanDisk to capture upside if market prices rise.

The financial scale of those agreements is the most important number in O'Malley's note: SanDisk has secured $42 billion in minimum contractual revenue and $11 billion in financial guarantees from these deals, TipRanks confirmed . That is not a cyclical trade. It is a contracted revenue floor that reduces the binary risk embedded in most memory stock calls.

Where Barclays sees SanDisk sitting in the memory market

"We see Memory/Storage as the most attractive vertical below accelerators," O'Malley said, according to CNBC . That framing positions SanDisk directly behind AI accelerators in terms of secular demand strength, a significant statement given how much investor attention the accelerator segment commands.

Story Continues

Barclays expects the supply-demand imbalance in memory to persist through 2027. The combination of contracted revenue floors, ongoing pricing strength, and AI-driven data growth is what O'Malley believes makes SanDisk's current valuation defensible even after its extraordinary run.

On the same day, Barclays also raised its Micron price target to $1,175 from $675, maintaining an Overweight rating. The simultaneous moves signal a broad view that the memory upcycle still has duration, not a one-stock call, according to TipRanks.

Key figures from Barclays' May 27 SanDisk note:

New target: $2,300, raised from $1,200; upgraded to Overweight from Equal-weight; analyst Tom O'Malley; 45% upside from $1,589.55 close, according to CNBC

Stock reaction: shares rose 7.5% on May 27; 52-week range $36.21 to $1,643.00; up more than 4,000% over the past twelve months, according to Investing.com

Contract structure: $42 billion in minimum contractual revenue; $11 billion in financial guarantees; longest contracts extend to 2031, according to TipRanks

Concurrent Micron call: Barclays raised Micron target to $1,175 from $675, Overweight maintained; O'Malley noted Micron signed its first five-year Strategic Customer Agreement, TipRanks confirmed

Street comparison: Melius Research holds Street-high target of $2,350; Citi raised to $2,025 from $1,300, implying approximately 27% upside, according to Investing.com

What investors should understand about the risk in this call

The $42 billion revenue floor changes the risk profile of this stock in a way that most memory calls cannot claim. Contracted minimum revenue through 2031 means SanDisk's earnings visibility is structurally higher than a traditional NAND cycle story. That is what justifies O'Malley's willingness to nearly double a price target on a stock already up 4,000%.

The remaining risk is execution.

Variable pricing in the back half of those contracts means SanDisk can capture upside if memory prices rise, but the floor pricing in the near term also limits how much of any near-term pricing spike flows through to earnings.

The contracts trade some near-term upside for long-term certainty, which is the right trade if you believe, as O'Malley does, that the cycle has more duration than the market expects.

For investors, the question is whether $2,300 is the right target for a contracted revenue model with $42 billion in guaranteed minimums, or whether that floor was already priced in by the market before Barclays published its note.

The 7.5% rally on the day suggests the market had not fully reflected it. O'Malley's 45% implied upside from that level suggests there is still room left to run.

Related: Citi revamps SanDisk stock price target for the rest of 2026

This story was originally published by TheStreet on May 27, 2026, where it first appeared in the Investing section. Add TheStreet as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Barclays expects the supply-demand imbalance in memory to persist through 2027Sullivan/Getty Images

Barclays expects the supply-demand imbalance in memory to persist through 2027Sullivan/Getty Images Where Barclays sees SanDisk sitting in the memory market

news.Title: 聯發科快追上高通 台達電市值破6.5兆 謝金河：從一人武林變群山效應
news.Content:
財信傳媒董事長謝金河27日在 臉書 感念已故好友范疇，驚嘆其五年前精準預測台灣將因國際變局成為「風雲國家」。如今台股市值超越印度，聯發科與台達電雙雙晉升兩千億美元級巨頭，不僅印證先知灼見，更宣告台灣已從「一人的武林」正式跨入壯闊的「群山效應」。

五年前預見台灣的先知灼見

謝金河27日在臉書發文表示，他在追思尹衍樑先生之際，也想起2023年11月辭世的好友范疇。回顧2020年疫情爆發年底，范疇曾鐵口直斷預測，中國在習近平引導下走上荒謬回頭路，而台灣則在習近平「幫助」下成為全球風雲國家。謝金河感嘆，五年後的今天檢視這番話，范疇堪稱先知，這兩天台股市值達4.95兆美元，已超車印度成為全球第五大股市，出口更有機會躋身全球前10大國，讓全世界都看到台灣。

聯發科挑戰高通轉型AI供應鏈

提到具體產業表現，謝金河指出聯發科股價持續大漲，市值衝上7.442兆台幣（約2366億美元），正快步追上高通。若與中國企業對標，聯發科已超越工業富聯與中國移動，排名可排在第12大。謝金河觀察到，聯發科已跳脫昔日手機晶片廠框架，搖身變成AI ASIC供應鏈成員，甚至進一步挑戰AVGO（博通），這種威力的呈現正逐漸加劇。

台達電與泰達電聯手再造傳奇

另一座世界級大山台達電也展現驚人實力。謝金河直言，台達電已是世界級HVDC大廠，市值達到6.5458兆台幣，讓昔日領先的鴻海現在連車尾燈都看不見。特別值得關注的是台達電子公司泰達電，在泰國地位猶如台積電在台灣，其市值達4.416兆泰銖，兩者加總超過10兆台幣。這股勢頭更帶動泰金寶股價今年漲幅接近100%，讓董事長許勝雄最近心情特別好。

台股群山效應正在崛起

除了台積電、聯發科與台達電這三座世界級大山，謝金河認為台灣正步入「群山效應」。聯電市值衝至1.8兆元，而被動元件龍頭陳泰銘的國巨也飆升至1.45兆元市值，助其登上首富寶座。謝金河轉述陳泰銘的感受，指他「直呼像夢境一般」，這正是台灣經濟實力的另一場傳奇演出。他最後總結，范疇五年前預見台灣成為風雲國家的藍圖，現在正一步步兌現中。

（封面圖／翻攝自謝金河臉書）

news.Title: The Quiet AI Winner That Doesn't Show Up on Any Hot Stock List
news.Content:
AI stock lists usually involve Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) and Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) , or perhaps Palantir (NASDAQ: PLTR) . Investors rarely think about a beaten-down telecom company that traded at $1 two years ago.

They should give Lumen Technologies (NYSE: LUMN) a chance, though.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

As of May 26, Lumen's stock has gained 416% in three years. It has nearly $13 billion in contracts with AI hyperscalers. Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) Web Services (AWS) and Anthropic are both building products around Lumen's networks. The stock formerly known as CenturyLink is up 170% in the past year alone.

So why isn't it on anyone's radar?

Because fiber is boring

Lumen doesn't make GPUs. It doesn't train large language models. CEO Kate Johnson doesn't even post cryptic memes on social media. But Lumen has nearly 80 million miles of fiber-optic cable connecting 139 data centers across North America. And each hub connects to the others over high-speed fiber at 400 gigabits per second.

That fiber is increasingly valuable. AI workloads move massive amounts of data between training clusters, inference servers, and multiple cloud providers. All of that data travels over physical infrastructure. Much of it travels over Lumen's infrastructure, especially over long distances.

The company has been quietly signing deals with the biggest names in tech. Recent clients include Microsoft, Anthropic, AWS, and Alphabet 's (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) Google Cloud. These aren't speculative partnerships. AWS just made Lumen the first network operator for its new Interconnect service .

The Alkira buyout

In May, Lumen announced a $475 million acquisition of Alkira, a software company specializing in cloud-to-cloud connectivity. This deal targets what the industry calls "East-West" traffic: data moving between data centers and cloud environments rather than from users to the cloud.

The deal should compress years of product development into months and reduce Lumen's capital expenses by $100 million to $200 million over time.

The turnaround story

Three years ago, Lumen was a mess. The stock had collapsed to around $1 per share. The company was bleeding cash, weighed down by legacy telecom services nobody wanted anymore. Value investors who'd bought the stock for its generous dividend got crushed when the dividend disappeared.

Story Continues

Then the AI boom gained steam. Suddenly, Lumen's massive fiber network wasn't an expensive liability. It was a strategic asset instead.

The company pivoted hard, signing what it calls "private connectivity fabric" deals with hyperscalers worth nearly $13 billion. It paid down some debt by raising cash from the sale of its residential fiber operations to AT&T (NYSE: T) .

Lumen wants to become the infrastructure layer for enterprise AI. One network, any cloud, total control. ( CEO Kate Johnson's words , not mine.)

The risks

Lumen's legacy revenue fell 3.2% year over year in Q1. The company is unprofitable, so price-to-earnings ratios don't make sense. Despite the recent paydown, Lumen's debt is still a crushing $17 billion. And the stock has already bounced back a lot, leaving less room for future gains.

But the valuation remains cheap at 0.9 times sales, and hyperscalers keep signing beefy checks. If you're looking for AI exposure that doesn't involve buying Nvidia at 21 times sales, Lumen is worth a look.

Just don't expect to see it on any "Top AI Stocks to Buy Now" listicle anytime soon.

news.Title: 【統一證券晨訊】指數有望持續新高
news.Content:
日期：2026年 5月28日

※盤勢分析

科技巨頭看好台灣AI，台股量價齊揚，指數有望持續新高

※昨日盤勢

受到美半指數大漲逾5.5%並創歷史新高的激勵下，帶動台股早盤以43,842點

開盤後即為今日最低點，隨後在AI權值龍頭、矽晶圓、被動元件、金融及券商股

的買盤湧入下，盤中一度創下44,818點的歷史新高。終場收在44,256點，大漲

731點，且成交量創下歷史新天量，顯示市場資金參與度極高，呈現價量齊揚的

走勢。權值股方面，台積電上漲1.32%，鴻海上漲1.93%，聯發科上漲8.79%，廣

達下跌1.42%，台達電上漲7.23%。

盤面強勢股，南茂、聯電、立隆電、群創、穎崴、jpp-KY、華邦電、弘塑、

致和證等漲停；國巨*、富邦金、聯發科、禾伸堂、台達電、可成、統一證、環

球晶、創新服務等漲幅6%以上；盤面弱勢股，森崴能源、中釉、遠東新、東台、

光聖、迎廣、直得、博磊、環宇-KY等跌停；神盾、川寶、華星光、確精、京元

電子、杰力、高鋒、貿聯-KY、尖點等跌幅6%以上。終場台灣加權指數上漲

731.43點，以44256.8點作收，成交量為1.63兆元。

觀察盤面變化，三大類股全面上漲，電子上漲1.85%、傳產上漲0.01%、金融

上漲2.12%。在次族群部份以塑膠、電子零組件、半導體表現較佳，分別上漲

3.31%、2.73%、2.17%。

※資金動向

三大法人合計買超307.78億元。其中外資買超381.28億元，投信賣超42.19

億元，自營商賣超31.31億元。外資買超前五大為力積電、富邦金、南亞、群創

、國泰金；賣超前五大為友達、台新新光金、遠東新、旺宏、長榮航。投信買超

前五大為台新新光金、長榮航、華南金、廣達、華邦電；賣超前五大為富邦金、

國泰金、遠東新、聯電、京元電子。資券變化方面，融資增16.76億元，融資餘

額為5362.69億元，融券增1.32萬張，融券餘額為24.16萬張。外資台指期部位，

空單增加1949口，淨空單部位56302口，借券賣出金額392.59億元。類股成交比

重：電子88.59%、傳產8.61%、金融2.80%。

※今日盤勢分析

美股昨夜表現呈分歧態勢。雖然道瓊與標普500受惠於油價下跌而續創歷史

新高，但費半修正1.36%。值得留意的是，台積電ADR逆勢上漲2.52%，這對台股

具有安定作用。產業方面，SpaceX取得美國太空軍巨額合約，對於台灣廠如華通

、昇達科等，具備資金吸引力。台股方面，昨日在AI電子權值股、被動元件與記

憶體等族群群起上攻下，終場收在44256.8點，創下收盤歷史新高，成交量更擴

增至歷史新天量1.63兆元，顯示市場資金動能相當充沛，外資亦呈現連續買超態

勢。整體而言，預估指數震盪盤堅，續創新高。

台股後市分析如下：第一、台積電近期傳出擬調整員工分紅比例，引發部分

員工反彈，甚至出現罷工聲浪。董事長魏哲家27日為此取消原訂海外出差行程，

親自主持內部溝通會。魏哲家在會中向員工信心喊話，強調今年員工分紅金額仍

將維持強勁成長，增幅至少不低於去年水準(30%)，並鼓勵員工買進自家股票，

直言「買台積電股票，保證未來生活無虞」。

第二、SpaceX於5月20日正式向美國證券交易委員會（SEC）遞交招股說明書

，確認將以 SPCX 為代號在那斯達克掛牌。市場預期最快於6月12日開始交易。

市場推估，SpaceX的估值區間落在1.75兆至2兆美元，募資規模則可能達到400億

至800億美元。若最終定價與需求符合預期，此次IPO有望成為全球史上最大規模

的首次公開募股。

第三、技術面觀察，周三大盤收了一根留有上影線的紅K棒，反映出高檔存

在部分的獲利回吐與調節壓力，但整體指數依然穩健運行於所有均線之上，多頭

排列格局尚未改變。指標方面，KD、RSI等短期指標持續震盪向上，且MACD柱體

正式由綠轉紅，顯示中期的多頭動能再度轉強，短線上仍具備向上挑戰的動能。

第四、盤面資金焦聚AI權值股、被動元件、券商股，包括：聯發科、台達電

、凱美、華新科、致和證、康和證等表現強勢。

※投資策略

黃仁勳表示將擴大在台投資、魏哲家直言買台積電保證生活無虞、聯發科看

好Agentic AI、加上COMPUTEX 開展在即、科技巨頭相繼訪台固樁供應鏈，眾利

多因素有望帶動台股續創新高。惟聯準會態度依舊謹慎，季線正乖離居高不下、

外資期貨淨空單逾5.6萬口，短線須提防高檔劇烈波動。操作上可保持適度的資

金彈性，建議分批布局AI權值股。長線追蹤族群：台積電及先進製程/封裝、高

速傳輸(含光傳輸)、載板與PCB、機箱滑軌、被動元件、功率半導體、航太衛星

、特用化學及漲價概念等族群。

news.Content:
【昨日盤勢】：

\n

【資金動向】：

三大法人合計買超307.78億元。其中外資買超381.28億元，投信賣超42.19億元，自營商賣超31.31億元。外資買超前五大為力積電、富邦金、南亞、群創、國泰金；賣超前五大為友達、台新新光金、遠東新、旺宏、長榮航。投信買超前五大為台新新光金、長榮航、華南金、廣達、華邦電；賣超前五大為富邦金、國泰金、遠東新、聯電、京元電子。資券變化方面，融資增16.76億元，融資餘額為5362.69億元，融券增1.32萬張，融券餘額為24.16萬張。外資台指期部位，空單增加1949口，淨空單部位56302口，借券賣出金額392.59億元。類股成交比重：電子88.59%、傳產8.61%、金融2.80%。

【今日盤勢分析】：

美股昨夜表現呈分歧態勢。雖然道瓊與標普500受惠於油價下跌而續創歷史新高，但費半修正1.36%。值得留意的是，台積電ADR逆勢上漲2.52%，這對台股具有安定作用。產業方面，SpaceX取得美國太空軍巨額合約，對於台灣廠如華通、昇達科等，具備資金吸引力。台股方面，昨日在AI電子權值股、被動元件與記憶體等族群群起上攻下，終場收在44256.8點，創下收盤歷史新高，成交量更擴增至歷史新天量1.63兆元，顯示市場資金動能相當充沛，外資亦呈現連續買超態勢。整體而言，預估指數震盪盤堅，續創新高。台股後市分析如下：

第一、台積電近期傳出擬調整員工分紅比例，引發部分員工反彈，甚至出現罷工聲浪。董事長魏哲家27日為此取消原訂海外出差行程，親自主持內部溝通會。魏哲家在會中向員工信心喊話，強調今年員工分紅金額仍將維持強勁成長，增幅至少不低於去年水準 (30%)，並鼓勵員工買進自家股票，直言「買台積電股票，保證未來生活無虞」。

第三、技術面觀察，周三大盤收了一根留有上影線的紅K棒，反映出高檔存在部分的獲利回吐與調節壓力，但整體指數依然穩健運行於所有均線之上，多頭排列格局尚未改變。指標方面，KD、RSI等短期指標持續震盪向上，且MACD柱體正式由綠轉紅，顯示中期的多頭動能再度轉強，短線上仍具備向上挑戰的動能。

第四、盤面資金焦聚AI權值股、被動元件、券商股，包括：聯發科、台達電、凱美、華新科、致和證、康和證等表現強勢。

【投資策略】：

黃仁勳表示將擴大在台投資、魏哲家直言買台積電保證生活無虞、聯發科看好Agentic AI、加上COMPUTEX 開展在即、科技巨頭相繼訪台固樁供應鏈，眾利多因素有望帶動台股續創新高。惟聯準會態度依舊謹慎，季線正乖離居高不下、外資期貨淨空單逾5.6萬口，短線須提防高檔劇烈波動。操作上可保持適度的資金彈性，建議分批布局AI權值股。長線追蹤族群：台積電及先進製程/封裝、高速傳輸(含光傳輸)、載板與PCB、機箱滑軌、被動元件、功率半導體、航太衛星、特用化學及漲價概念等族群。
